5cf1be1c013412aa.title: Auditing | University of Tübingen
5cf1be1c013412aa.text: Application for guest students People with an appropriate level of education may apply to sit in on classes at the University if there is free capacity. You do not necessarily need to have completed high school, but be aware that the vast majority of classes at the University of Tubingen take place in German. The application for the guest auditor permit can be submitted by post or as a scan by e-mail ( studierendensekretariat spam prevention @zv.uni-tuebingen.de ). The application deadline for the summer semester is no later than April 15 (date of receipt by post) and for the winter semester no later than October 15 (date of receipt by post). The fee for guest students is currently € 50 per semester. If you want to attend seminars, practical courses oder exercises, the approval of the faculty concerned is required in each case. In any case, please send the guest auditor application (see below) to the student secretary. For details of University of Tubingen classes, you can search the online course catalogue: alma Portal . In case of participation in online courses. Please ask the respective university teacher which access you can use to attend the lecture online. The access will then be set up for you by the university lecturer, the respective institute or the Zentrum für Datenverarbeitung. Application form Fee Before your application can be approved, you must transfer the guest student fee of €50 to the following account: Empfänger: Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen IBAN: DE24 6005 0101 7477 5036 14 Baden-Württembergische Bank AG BIC: SOLADEST600 In the transfer (Verwendungszweck), include your given name, family name and the addition "Gasthoerer" Please note : As a guest student you are not a member of the University of Tübingen and are not entitled to attend language courses at the Language Learning Center. ILIAS access: Guest students receive access to ILIAS upon request.

06d23902b098f1e1.title: Student ID-card | University of Tübingen
headings: Loss of the student ID card
You can find charging stations for copy credit here
Here you will find a current overview (also with the locations outside Tübingen).
06d23902b098f1e1.text: Student ID / chip card Every student at the University of Tübingen receives a personal student ID in the form of a chip card from the Registrar's Office after enrolment. This student ID card currently fulfills the following functions: Function as a student ID card, e.g. for ticket inspections. You can renew or update the semester imprint yourself at the self-service terminals . Access control with key function in various buildings. Payment function - rechargeable - at public charging stations in the campus area - for cashless payment in the canteen and at the photocopiers. Further information can be found here https://www.my-stuwe.de/mensa/bezahlung/#nav-bezahlen QR code : The functions of the QR code are currently still being developed. If the code is read, the value “non-student or unrecorded student” is incorrectly displayed. As soon as the QR code can be used correctly, we will inform you on this website. Viewing the card data at the self-service terminals Loss of the student ID card This chip card is the property of the University of Tübingen. If it is lost or damaged due to careless behavior, we will have to charge you the corresponding fees (currently € 15). In the event of card defects due to technical malfunctions (e.g. the semester imprint is no longer visible), you will of course receive a replacement free of charge on presentation of the defective card. Please note that in the event of theft or loss of the chip card, no replacement/payout of the credit balance is possible! In the event of theft or loss, the Registrar's Office can block your student ID card. To do this, please send us the signed application for a replacement card and transfer the €15 to the account of the Universitätskasse Baden-Württembergische Bank AG, IBAN: DE29 6005 0101 7477 5036 21, reciver (Empfänger) Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen. Please state only your student number as the reason for payment. Once your card has been blocked by the Registrar's Office, the Studierendenwerk will also be automatically informed overnight to block the payment function and the University Library to block the loan function, and the card will be blocked for access to the building. You can obtain the application form required for a replacement card here. You can send old, defective ID cards together with this application form here or drop them in your letterbox. A new passport photo is not required for the creation of a replacement ID card, but can be used on request. It takes about a week to create a replacement ID card. The new ID card will then be sent to you by post. If you find student ID cards in the university area, please send them to the Registrar's Office by internal mail or drop them in the internal mailbox in Wilhelmstraße 19. The Registrar's Office will then send the student ID cards to the students after checking them. Note : The new ID cards have a matt white strip at the bottom of the front for the semester imprint. The chip required for the function is not visible to you under the plastic layer. Updating the semester imprint Updating the semester imprint You can update the semester imprint on your student ID card (chip card) at the self-service terminals once you have re-registered. The self-service terminals can be found at the following locations: University Library Wilhelmstraße 32 ground floor (accessible during building opening hours) and in the Morgenstelle Lecture Hall Center (accessible during building opening hours). Cashless payment in the canteen Cashless payment in the canteen You can use your student ID to make cashless payments in the canteen of the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim AöR. Further information can be found on the website of the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim AöR . Copy function Copy function If the chip card is lost, the copying credit cannot be replaced. A payment of the copying credit or transfer to the new card or a check of the functionality of the copying function is only possible via the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim AöR. There is a service point on the ground floor of Mensa Wilhelmstraße and one in Mensa Morgenstelle. You can find the current opening hours on this page. And for all technical questions and problems with copying, please contact the Morgenstern company: Firma Morgenstern - Servicebüro für Druck- und Kopieren: Office hours: Mon-Fri 11-14 Room A 326 in the Ammerbau of the University Library (access through the main building/textbook collection) Telephone service: 07071 29 74625 Mon-Fri 9-18 and Sat 9-14 You can find charging stations for copy credit here You can find charging stations for copy credit here Locations of the EC revaluators Mensa Wilhelmstraße 13 Mensa Morgenstelle, Cafeteria Hörsaalzentrum und Cafeteria Morgenstelle Mensa Prinz Karl Hafengasse 6 Cafeteria Neuphilologicum im Brechtbau Wilhelmstraße 50 Cafeteria Clubhaus Wilhelmstraße 30 Cafeteria Theologicum Liebermeisterstraße 12 Automat Auf dem Sand 14 Wohnheimverwaltung WHO, Fichtenweg 5 Sportinsitut Alberstraße 27 Französisches Viertel Wohnheim Wankheimer Täle 9 Universitätsbibliothek Wilhelmstraße 32 Neue Aula Geschwister-Scholl-Platz Here you will find a current overview (also with the locations outside Tübingen). Access function Access function Building access with the chip card is activated either automatically or by the respective building representative. If errors occur in the access function in just one individual building, please contact the respective supervisor of this area first. In the ZDV (Zentrum für Datenverarbeitung), the university's central computer center, this would be the user secretariat at Wächterstraße 76, for example. Do not allow anyone else to pass through appropriately secured doors with your ID card - even if this person has a valid ID card! In the event of misuse, your access will be blocked! Malfunctions at the self-service terminals Malfunctions at the self-service terminals In the event of faults at the self-service terminals in the University Library, please contact the staff at the information desk on the second floor. In case of malfunctions at the self-service terminals in the Morgenstelle Lecture Hall Center, please contact the janitors or the staff of the building management there. International Student Identity Card ISIC International Student Identity Card ISIC The online service and further information on the International Student Identity Card ISIC (including the issuing offices) can be found at: https://www.isic.de/. Error messages at the terminals Error messages at the terminals Delete card The message “Delete card” is displayed if the card was removed too quickly from a reader and the booking could not be completed. This then causes the card to be blocked. Remedy 1: Go to the device where the payment was apparently the last to work. Insert the card there and wait briefly. This will then be corrected automatically. If the device can no longer be found: Remedy 2: Go to the info point of the Studierendenwerk www.my-stuwe.de/mensa/infopoints/ and have the card read out. The staff there can see where the blockage occurred and can tell you the exact location or, if necessary, unblock the wallet. The card should then work again without any restrictions. Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

7adbdedc457b989f.title: Switching programs | University of Tübingen
headings: Applications and change of study program
What you should do if changing study programs
Transfer to a Master´s degree program
Switching to a second or higher semester
Changing your program when you are a doctoral candidate
7adbdedc457b989f.text: Switching programs If you are already enrolled at the University of Tübingen and want to change your target degree, your subject, or one of your subjects (in combined degree programs) - then you will need to make an official application to change your study program. If you change programs, you do not continue with your current course of studies (unless you switch from a Bachelor’s directly into a Master’s program in the corresponding subject before you can present a Bachelor’s degree certificate). If you want to continue with your previous studies, they become a parallel degree course. Please note: We can only process your transfer application once you have re-registered for the relevant semester with your previous degree program. For further information please visit: parallel degree course Application for change of program: Application for transfer in undergraduate programs (e. g. bachelor, state exam etc.) Application for transfer in Master´s programs Application for transfer in doctorate Applications and change of study program Applications and change of study program If you aim to switch to a restricted-admission degree program, you must apply for it prior to the application deadline and obtain a new admission notice. You must apply for a change of study program once you have received this new admission notice - not before. Ensure that you apply for your change of program before the date set out in your admission notice. Your application for change of program must also include your admission notice. You do not have to apply in advance for a change of program to a subject with no special requirements for admission. You can switch to such a degree program at Student Administration by 30 September for the following winter semester and by 31 März for the following summer semester. If you want to switch to a Master program / switch Master programs, you always have to apply first! Please note: You cannot switch programs for past semesters. What you should do if changing study programs What you should do if changing study programs We recommend you get advice from your desired subject’s academic counselling service . You must prove that you have attended academic counselling by your desired subject if you are in a Bachelor’s program and are seeking to switch in your third or higher semester (§ 60 paragraph (2)(5) LHG). Template for certificate of attendance at academic counselling For your change of study program to be processed, Student Administration must have received all fees payable by you. Please note that bank transfers may take up to a week. If you have not re-enrolled within the mandatory re-enrollment period you will also have to pay an additional late fee. Transfer to a Master´s degree program Transfer to a Master´s degree program Basic requirement for enrollment in a master degree program is a completed bachelor degree (or any equivalent basic university degree). Please note: It is necessary to apply for every Master program before you can transfer. In case you cannot submit your diploma/certificate due to correction period you can apply for conditional temporary enrollment. Therefore please fill in the following form and submit it to our office; in addition to the regular application form for enrollment and the other documents. Form conditional temporary enrollment Switching to a second or higher semester Switching to a second or higher semester If you have completed assessment in your old program which may be accredited in your new one (this is the case if, e.g., you are studying a subject with the target degree Bachelor of Arts or Bachelor of Science and seek to change to a target degree of Bachelor of Education in that same subject) - you need a certificate of accreditation when you apply to switch programs. You can find information on the switch in higher semesters in restricted-admission subjects here . Template for certificate of accreditation (DE) If your previous study program had no special requirements for admission, you can get the certificate of accreditation either from the subject-related counselling service or the examinations office, depending on the faculty. Overview of subject counsellors Central examinations office Overview of other / external examinations offices Changing your program when you are a doctoral candidate Changing your program when you are a doctoral candidate Doctoral students may change programs any time for the current semester and, after the re-enrollment period has begun, for the following semester. The prerequisites for this are documented proof that the faculty has accepted you as a doctoral student, and a Master’s or equivalent degree. A change of program in the current semester is valid for the entire semester. You should therefore check with your examinations office and your health insurer to find out about any consequences of the switch.

e3176c79fa2e99fb.title: Leave of absence | University of Tübingen
headings: Application deadline for winter semester: 12 October 2026
Reasons for a leave of absence
What should be taken into account during a leave of absence
Students of the state examination programs in medicine, dentistry and pharmacy please note
e3176c79fa2e99fb.text: Leave of absence We accept completed and signed applications for leave of absence, which you send scanned by e-mail to studierendensekretariat@zv.uni-tuebingen.de. Application deadline for winter semester: 12 October 2026 As a rule, the period of leave of absence should not exceed t w o semesters. Please note that the leave of absence is by nature limited in time or tailored to recognizably temporary circumstances and cannot be granted permanently. The application should be made during the re-enrollment period for the summer semester: from 15 January to 15 February for the winter semester: from 1 June to 15 August but generally at the latest before the beginning of the respective lecture period of a semester: for the winter semester 2026: by 12 october 2026 at the latest for the summer semester 2026: by 13 april 2026 at the latest Only if the important reason for the leave of absence occurs later, without this being foreseeable, the application shall be submitted to the student administration office immediately after the occurrence of the unforeseen event occurs during the lecture period, with additional justification. Reasons for a leave of absence Reasons for a leave of absence Upon their application, students may be exempted from the obligation to study properly for good cause (leave of absence). For details and a full list of the reasons for leave of absence, please refer to the application for leave. You can find more information on the subject of leave of absence by clicking on the following links: Admission and enrollment regulations of the university (§ 14 leave of absence) Handouts from the family office for study guidance and examination offices (Studying with family respnsibilities) No leave of absence is possible for the following reasons: Exam preparation, avoiding exam deadlines, financial hardship, earning money, moving house, renovating, building a house, taking a holiday in the literal sense of the word, family celebrations of all kinds. Please note: A leave of absence for a study stay at a foreign university is not possible. With the leave of absence you are released from the student rights and obligations, i.e. you cannot, for example, take part in elections or be elected, nor can you perform any exam-relevant academic work. Examination deadlines are normally suspended during the leave of absence. Students with child and care responsibilities are entitled to participate in courses and to perform study and examination work. What should be taken into account during a leave of absence What should be taken into account during a leave of absence According to the State Law on Higher Education, a leave of absence should normally not exceed two semesters. This means that a leave of absence for a third semester is only possible in extreme exceptional cases. For processing, the application for leave of absence and the corresponding proofs (e.g. medical certificates, proof of internship, etc.) are required. The clerks in the Student administration office are responsible for processing. In order to process the application for leave of absence, it is necessary that the fees and contributions due despite the leave of absence have been paid and registered by the student administration office. Payment shall be made by transfer of the fees and contributions due for the re-registration. Students on leave of absence must also generally pay the administrative fee, the student services fee and fee for the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim AöR per semester. According to the admission and enrollment regulations of the University of Tübingen , a leave of absence for previous semesters is excluded. Registration and immediate leave of absence is not possible! Student teachers: The compulsory internship prescribed by the new examination regulations is already included in the standard period of study, which is why no leave of absence is possible. Upon written application, the leave of absence can be cancelled for the current semester. The application must be submitted by the end of the lecture period at the latest. If necessary, please contact your clerk at the Student administration office directly and clarify the necessary formalities with him or her. Unfortunately, the student department cannot provide information about the consequences of a leave of absence for BAföG or child benefit. Note on statutory accident insurance protection: During an internship you are not insured against accidents by the university. The reason is that you are leaving the university's area of responsibility and organization for the duration of the internship. Therefore, please clarify before your leave of absence whether you are subject to statutory accident insurance for your internship. If this is not the case, you would otherwise have to take out private insurance if necessary. Please note that a leave of absence for one semester can lead to problems in the further course of studies for subjects with annual operation. In the case of the annual programme, the first semester of study can only ever begin in the winter semester. Lectures for the even subject semesters are therefore only offered in the summer semester. For example, you are in the 3rd semester of the winter semester and want to take a leave of absence for the following summer semester. You will then be in your 4th semester after the leave of absence in the following winter semester. However, lectures for the fourth semester are only offered in the summer semester. Therefore, before taking a leave of absence, please enquire at the student advisory service whether and if so, which problems the leave of absence here may cause for you. Here you will find an overview of the study advisors. Students of the state examination programs in medicine, dentistry and pharmacy please note Students of the state examination programs in medicine, dentistry and pharmacy please note that the minimum study periods or semesters specified in the respective licensing regulations must be proven before admission to the respective state examinations can be granted. Leave semesters do not count towards the minimum study periods, regardless of the university credits and examinations taken in them. Application form Application for leave of absence for the winter semester 2026/2027: to be submitted by 12 october 2026 at the latest Application for leave of absence for the summer semester 2026: to be submitted by 13 april 2026 at the latest

0302cb794892b669.title: Re-enrolling | University of Tübingen
headings: June 1 – August 15 for re-enrollment in the following winter semester
January 15 –February 15 for re-enrollment in the following summer semester.
0302cb794892b669.text: Re-enrolling If you are enroled at the University of Tübingen, have not yet finished your degree, and plan to continue your studies next semester, you must re-enroll within the designated re-enrollment period. The details required for re-enrollment are sent to all students’ University e-mail accounts when the re-enrolment period begins. The re-enrollment period is usually June 1 – August 15 for re-enrollment in the following winter semester January 15 –February 15 for re-enrollment in the following summer semester. You may pay your re-enrollment fees by bank transfer to the following account: Baden-Württembergische Bank unselbstständige Anstalt der Landesbank Baden-Württemberg Kleiner Schlossplatz 11, 70173 Stuttgart International bank transfers: BIC: SOLADEST600 and IBAN: DE29 6005 0101 7477 5036 21 Recipient: Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen The transfer must be accompanied by your enrollment number – nothing else. Fees must be paid into the University account by the end of the re-enrollment period. Fees paid after the closing date will incur a fine of €10. After re-enrolling, you can extend the validity of your chipcard (adding the new semester to the card) at the terminals available. You can check your current target and actual amounts at any time under ALMA . There you will also receive all certificates as PDF files after the re-enrollment. If you fail to re-enrol for the winter semester by September 30 or for the summer semester by March 31, and you have not canceled your enrolment ( exmatrikulieren ) your enrollment will be canceled by the University.

7d5b2dcb39a3a4f0.title: Termination of enrollment | University of Tübingen
headings: Access to electronic services after de-registration
7d5b2dcb39a3a4f0.text: Termination of enrollment If you are a student and wish to terminate your enrollment at the University of Tübingen, you can do so at any time via Student Administration. Students usually terminate their enrollment when they finish their studies (having successfully completed a final examination). If you have finished your studies but do not actively take this step, we will terminate your enrollment automatically at the next end of semester (March 31 for winter semester or September 30 for summer semester). You cannot terminate your enrollment retroactively to a date in the past. You can apply now to terminate your enrollment for the end of the semester . If you will soon finish your studies and wish to terminate your enrollment, e.g. to avoid paying the fees, please check first with your examinations office; you may lose your right to be examined if you terminate your enrollment too soon. Student Administration cannot answer this question. Access to electronic services after de-registration You will still have access to ALMA and the student email address for six weeks after the de-registration date. The ZDV will then inform you by email a few days before the expiry date about the binding end date. After this date, access is no longer possible and your stored data will be deleted. On the other hand, you only have access to many external services that the university charges for until the date of de-registration. After this date, access will be blocked without further notice. The examiners and examination offices have access to the student data even after de-registration and can then continue to record achievements, issue ToR and certificates. Termination of enrollment application Student Administration must receive an application for termination of enrollment from you - including a discharge note from the University Library confirming that you have no outstanding fees or unreturned materials (Entlastungsvermerk). Please use this online application whenever possible ( * are mandatory fields). Your application will be sent directly to the University Library. If all fees have been paid and media returned, the Library will then forward the application to Student Administration. From there you will receive your termination of enrollment notice. Termination of enrollment application The form validation failed, please fill in all required fields. Exmatrikulation EN Student ID number* * Given name, family name* * Street, no.* * Additional address info (e.g. room no.) Postal code and town* * Country, if not Germany Private Email address: Please provide a personal email address so we can stay in touch once your student email address is no longer active * Termination of enrollment for the following reason* * studies ended after final exams passed examinations not yet completed studies ended without exams completed because exam not possible change of university conscription to military or civilian service abandonment or interruption of studies studies ended after exam failed on final attempt other reasons Exmatrikulation * end of summer semester 2026 on 30 September 2026 end of winter semester 2025/2026 on 31 March 2026 end of summer semester 2025 on 30 September 2025 with legal effect as of (see next line - today at the earliest) Termination of enrollment legally effective as of Note Alumni contact after graduation We will retain your email address to maintain our connection with our graduates (legal basis: Section 12(1) LHG BW). You may object to this use at any time. Alumni-Contact I object to the use of my email address for maintaining alumni relations (sec. 12(1) LHG BW). Don't fill this field! You will need a termination of enrollment notice if you wish to enroll at another university. You do not need to submit any other documents for termination of enrollment. You may keep your student ID card as a momento. Access to online services following termination of enrollment You will still have access to ALMA and your student email address for one month after your enrollment termination date. The IT Center (ZDV) will then inform you by email of the date on which your access will end. However, you only have access to Illias and many external services for which the University charges a fee until two weeks after your termination of enrollment date. After that, access will be blocked without further notice. Further information on termination of enrollment If you still have copy credit, you can have it paid out at the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim Servicepoint at the canteen (Mensa). Details here: Servicepoint address and opening hours . The administration fee of €70 can only be refunded if the student terminates enrollment no later than one month after the start of the lecture period . For this, a fully completed application for termination of enrollment must be received by Student Administration (cutoff date!). If you terminate your enrollment before the start of the semester, we will automatically transfer the €70 back to your account. If your bank details have changed or you have paid in cash, please inform us in writing of your current bank details so that repayment can go ahead. If you terminate your enrollment after the start of the semester, you must apply for repayment in writing: Application for refund of tution fee / administration fee If you have already paid the semester fee (currently €84.80) for the next semester, the following applies for repayment: If you terminate your enrollment by the end of the previous semester, we will automatically transfer the money back to your account. If you terminate your enrollment after the start of the semester, this money may be refunded by the Studierendenwerk Tübingen-Hohenheim in certain cases upon written application. Details are available in the Student Services regulations . A copy of the termination of enrollment notice must be submitted with the application. The contact person is Ms. Wolf Student Services Tübingen-Hohenheim AöR , Friedrichstraße 21, 72072 Tübingen, phone: 07071 29 73846, Email: buchhaltung spam prevention @sw-tuebingen-hohenheim.de If you have already paid the €12 fee for the student union, the following applies for repayment: If you terminate your enrollment before the end of the previous semester, we will automatically transfer the money back to your account. If you terminate your enrollment after the start of the semester, this money may be refunded in certain cases upon written application. Please contact the Verfassten Studierendenschaft (Student Union) directly. If you do not want to or cannot use our online application, you can find the printable application here: Exmatrikulationsantrag Application for termination of enrollment You can send the application (completed PDF without signature or scan or photo) by email to the University Library, preferably from your student email address: You can also go to the University Library during opening hours to obtain the discharge note or send the application to the Universitätsbibliothek, Leihstelle, Postfach 2620, 72016 Tübingen. If you have no outstanding fees or unreturned media, the University Library will then add a discharge note to your application and forward the application to Student Administration. You will then receive your termination of enrollment notice by post from Student Administration. The University of Tübingen wishes you all the best for the future. If you wish to remain associated with the University of Tübingen, you have two options: UTA Universität Tübingen Alumni, Rümelinstr. 27, 72070 Tübingen, email or Membership in the Friends of the University of Tübingen, Universitätsbund (Vereinigung der Freunde der Universität Tübingen e.V.) , Wilhelmstraße 7, 72074 Tübingen, Telefax 07071 29 5995, Email . Contact Please state your application no. or student ID number, if available. Student Administration Postal address Wilhelmstrasse 19 72074 Tübingen, Germany Phone: +49 7071 29-74444 Monday - Friday: 8.30 - 11.30 a.m. Monday - Thursday: 13.00 - 15.00 a.m. Send Email Fax: +49 7071 29-5550 Office hours: General office hours

643b7a72ddb686c2.title: Media relations | University of Tübingen
643b7a72ddb686c2.text: Guide to: Media relations Expand all Press Releases Press Releases The Public Relations Department issues press releases on events and important developments at the University of Tübingen. It supports university members in the preparation and distribution of press releases. However, not every topic is suitable for a press release – we will be happy to advise on which communication channel is suitable for your news. Possible reasons for a press release could be, for example: Current research results, major events, invitations to press conferences, prominent guests. Important: Please contact us at least ten days before the planned publication date so that the press release can be sent to the media on time. As a basis for a press release, we need a first draft. We are also happy to include suitable image material for which you have the media rights. Press conferences Press conferences We organize press conferences for and with you. We send out invitations and arrange the venue and the equipment. Media representatives work under constant time pressure. So press conferences should only be held for selected topics – we will be happy to advise you on whether your topic is suitable. Press conferences are feasible, for example, for spectacular research results and major events. Important: Please contact us at least two weeks before the event so that we can advise and support you with your communication measures. Interview requests Interview requests A journalist would like to interview you as an expert on a specific topic? We are happy to advise you in the case of controversial topics or if you are unsure about giving an interview. Other communication channels Other communication channels If your topic is not suitable for a press release, the university has other channels through which news can be communicated The online news portal attempto online Newsletter Uni Tübingen aktuell (sent to both University members and external subscribers) Social Media Circulars Internal Newsletter (University employees only) Contact Do you have any questions? Contact the responsible member of the Public Relations Department. The Media Contact Other areas of responsibility Media Press Releases

bfd96eafd873762a.title: Media contact | University of Tübingen
bfd96eafd873762a.meta_description: Our press contacts will be happy to help you find suitable experts and provide information and images on topics related to the university.
bfd96eafd873762a.text: Media contact Our responsibilities include press and media relations, finding experts for the media, arranging filming permits, and producing the University’s Annual Report and the research magazine ATTEMPTO!. If you have any questions or enquiries, please write to presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de . Stefan Bentele Media relations +49 7071 29-76724 stefan.bentele spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Janna Eberhardt Media relations, research reporting +49 7071 29-77853 janna.eberhardt spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Michael Pfeiffer Media relations, annual report +49 7071 29-76782 michael.pfeiffer spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Tilman Wörtz Media scout, ATTEMPTO! magazine +49 7071 29-78622 tilman.woertz spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Nora Zeller Public relations & press office, digital communication +49 7071 29-77354 nora.zeller spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Director of Public Relations Christfried Dornis Director +49 7071 29-76788 christfried.dornis spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Other areas of responsibility Press Releases Social Media

0ee62274ffff6cca.title: Media | University of Tübingen
0ee62274ffff6cca.meta_description: Looking for information about the university, pictures or suitable experts? We are happy to support media representatives!
0ee62274ffff6cca.text: Media Do you have a press inquiry or would you like to report on the University of Tübingen? Are you looking for an expert on a specific topic? Do you need visual material on the University of Tübingen? We can put you in touch with scientists for a discussion or interview, provide you with information and picture material. We would also be happy to add you to our distribution list for press releases. And we are your contact if you need permission to film. Media Contact An overview of the contact persons. Find out more Photo pool for journalists Selection for reporting. Find out more Expand all Distribution lists Distribution lists Our press releases provide information on current research and key developments at the University. Representatives of the media are welcome to join our distribution list; contact presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de . There is a choice of the following distribution lists: Regional distribution list: All University press releases including events and regional topics. Supra-regional distribution list: Press releases on selected topics on matters of supra-regional significance, such as relevant research findings. There is also the possibility for journalists to get confidential access to embargoed press releases. Please note that we must ensure that this information is not published before the embargo ends. If you wish to be included on the distribution list of embargoed press releases, you must fill in and sign the following form " Verbindliche Zusage zur Einhaltung von Sperrfristen auf Inhalte von Pressemitteilungen der Universität " and send it to: University of Tübingen, Public Relations Department, Doblerstr. 21, 72074 Tübingen, Germany. Finding an expert Finding an expert Are you looking for an expert in a particular field? The Public Relations Department is happy to help media representatives to find the right contact person. Contact presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de As a comprehensive research university with more than 280 study programs, Tübingen covers nearly every area of academic study (with the exception of engineering). An overview of our core research and profile areas here: Core Research areas Profile Areas Permission to film Permission to film Are you planning to make video recordings on our campuses? You can apply for a permit to: presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de . Please supply the following information: What is the subject? For which medium would you be filming? Where and when do you want to film? How long will it take? We can help you find an appropriate setting. Please note that teaching and students’ studies may not be disrupted. 39ce7da62bf9e5d9.meta_description: Looking for information about the university, pictures or suitable experts? We are happy to support media representatives!
39ce7da62bf9e5d9.text: Media Do you have a press inquiry or would you like to report on the University of Tübingen? Are you looking for an expert on a specific topic? Do you need visual material on the University of Tübingen? We can put you in touch with scientists for a discussion or interview, provide you with information and picture material. We would also be happy to add you to our distribution list for press releases. And we are your contact if you need permission to film. Media Contact An overview of the contact persons. Find out more Photo pool for journalists Selection for reporting. Find out more Expand all Distribution lists Distribution lists Our press releases provide information on current research and key developments at the University. Representatives of the media are welcome to join our distribution list; contact presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de . There is a choice of the following distribution lists: Regional distribution list: All University press releases including events and regional topics. Supra-regional distribution list: Press releases on selected topics on matters of supra-regional significance, such as relevant research findings. There is also the possibility for journalists to get confidential access to embargoed press releases. Please note that we must ensure that this information is not published before the embargo ends. If you wish to be included on the distribution list of embargoed press releases, you must fill in and sign the following form " Verbindliche Zusage zur Einhaltung von Sperrfristen auf Inhalte von Pressemitteilungen der Universität " and send it to: University of Tübingen, Public Relations Department, Doblerstr. 21, 72074 Tübingen, Germany. Finding an expert Finding an expert Are you looking for an expert in a particular field? The Public Relations Department is happy to help media representatives to find the right contact person. Contact presse spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de As a comprehensive research university with more than 280 study programs, Tübingen covers nearly every area of academic study (with the exception of engineering).
