112bc6994811d2dc.text: Expand Download Modell eines Auslegerbootes "vaka lasi" Previous Next Name Modell Modell eines Auslegerbootes "vaka lasi" Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 2. Hälfte 20. Jh. Description Das Bootsmodell entspricht ziemlich genau den nur gepaddelten Auslegerbooten, wie sie im Bereich der nördlichen Inseln von Tuvalu üblich sind (Nanumea, Nanumanga, Niutao, Nui), allmählich aber auch auf den südlichen Atollen von Tuvalu übernommen werden. Es ist sehr sorgfältig gearbeitet und wurde mit allem üblichen Zubehör (Bonito-Angel, langes Kescher-Netz für fliegende Fische, Paddel, Ölfässer) ausgestattet, der teilweise mit einzelnen Kokosfasern an dem Boot befestigt ist. Auch die für das Boot notwendigen Bindungen wurden mit Kokosfasern oder aus gedrehter Kokoss chnur gemacht. headings: Kette "tui fafetu" mit rundem Zierstück aus Funafuti
e159a48abfb98c98.text: Expand Download Kette "tui fafetu" mit rundem Zierstück aus Funafuti Previous Next Name Schmuck Kette "tui fafetu" mit rundem Zierstück aus Funafuti Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 1987 Description Der Anhänger der Kette "tui fafetu" besteht aus einem inneren Reifen mit in Naturfarben eingefärbten Pandanusblatt-Oberhaut-Streifen, an den ein Kranz gelblich-weißer einfacher Seeschnecken mit Kokosfiederblatt-Faden angeknüpft wurde. Die Kette besteht aus Seeschnecken und Seemuschelschalen. Dieses Schmuckstück wurde Dr. Volker Harms, dem ehemaligen Kustos der ethnologischen Sammlung, als Abschiedsgeschenk zum Ende seiner Feldforschung in Funafuti um den Hals gelegt. 76368348d4c30829.text: Expand Download Maske für den "Tatanua"-Tanz Previous Next CC BY-NC-SA Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT / V. Marquardt Name Ritualgegenstand Maske für den "Tatanua"-Tanz Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date um 1900 Description "Malanggan" heißen in Mittel- und Nordneuirland sowie auf einigen dazu gehörenden kleineren Inseln sowohl die Schnitzwerke als auch die Totengedenkfeiern, für die sie hergestellt werden. In diesem Kontext wurde auch die gezeigte "Tatanua"-Maske verwendet. Die Tänze der "Tatanua" Maskenträger werden Teil eines Malanggan-Festes, wenn die Festveranstalter einen anderen Clan, zu dem in der Regel eine verwandtschaftliche Beziehung besteht, um die Aufführung eines solchen Tanzes bitten. 1de27afe564b344a.meta_description: Die Bemalung des Rindenbaststoffes teilt sich in drei quadratische Felder, um die ringsum eine Bordüre mit Ornamenten läuft. Das erste Feld zeigt eine durch gegenständ …
headings: Wandbehang mit gegenständlicher Verzierung aus Polynesien
1de27afe564b344a.text: Expand Download Images(2) Wandbehang mit gegenständlicher Verzierung aus Polynesien Previous Next Images(2) Name Textil Wandbehang mit gegenständlicher Verzierung aus Polynesien Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 1987 Description Die Bemalung des Rindenbaststoffes teilt sich in drei quadratische Felder, um die ringsum eine Bordüre mit Ornamenten läuft. Das erste Feld zeigt eine durch gegenständliche Ausmalungen (Seeschnecken, Fische, Korallen, Vögel und ein Auslegerboot) ergänzte Landkarte der Inseln Futuna und Alofi. Das zentrale Feld eine leicht stilisierte Fest-Szene: In der Mitte sitzt ein geschmückter Mann auf einer Matte vor einer Kava-Schałe und bereitet darin dieses Getränk; vor ihm singen und tanzen drei ebenfalls geschmückte Männer auf einem unter ihren Füßen angedeuteten Rasen. Neben dem Kava-Bereiter auf gleicher Höhe sind links eine Kokospalme und rechts eine Taropflanze jeweils willkürlich proportioniert gemalt. Im rechten Feld ist eine ebenfalls durch gegenständliche Ausmalungen (Seeschnecken, Korallen, Fisch und Vogel) ergänzte Karte der Insel Uvea (Wallis) zu sehen. 61318d509ba53bcf.title: Tapa-Briefmappe "siapo" – Works – eMuseum
61318d509ba53bcf.meta_description: Die Briefmappe besteht aus drei Lagen verschiedenen Materials, die fest miteinander vernäht wurden. Die erste innere Lage ist ein in Diagonal-Flechttechnik aus ca. 0,5 …
61318d509ba53bcf.text: Expand Download Images(2) Tapa-Briefmappe "siapo" Previous Next Images(2) Name Haushalt Tapa-Briefmappe "siapo" Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 1987 Description Die Briefmappe besteht aus drei Lagen verschiedenen Materials, die fest miteinander vernäht wurden. Die erste innere Lage ist ein in Diagonal-Flechttechnik aus ca. 0,5 cm breiten Pandanusblattstreifen hergestelltes Mattenstück. Die zweite Schicht besteht aus Rindenbaststoff, der in der üblichen dekorativen Manier mit aus Ruß hergestellter schwarzer Farbe ornamental bemalt wurde. Die dritte äußere Schicht bildet die importierte dünne, aber steife Klarsichtfolie. Sie wurde um die Kanten herum gefaltet, und dann wurden die verschiedenen Schichten und Einzelteile der Mappe ebenfalls importierten Kunststoff-Faserstreifen mit einem Dreiecksstich zusammengenäht. Dimensions L x B: 55 × 30 cm Medium Rindenbaststoff, Padanusblatt-Geflecht, Klarsichtfolie, Kunststoff-Faser Object number AOI-Es-A1386 Herkunft Kolopelu, Polynesien, Ozeanien Discover More Gebasteltes 2D Theater mit Märchen Thema und Figuren zum einschieben Nachkriegsjahre bis 1960er Seirocrinus subangularis Pi Chacán Primary Maker: Fernando de la Jara 2001 Stenopterygius quadriscissus (Reptilia, Ichthyosauria) Tapa-Handtasche "siapo" 1987 Unterschiedliche „Knirscher-Schienen“ 2021 E3 Spektrograph mit Quarzprisma Gyraulus elegans Gyraulus intermedius Gyraulus oxystoma Gyraulus planorbiformis Gyraulus sulcatus Gyraulus trochiformis Schubkurbeleinheit 1998–1999 Beuchet-Stuhl 1998 Dinosaurierfährte von Iguanodon Ichthyosaurier (Stenopterygius) Trapezoides Webgewicht Webstuhlgewicht Vorwärm-Kugel Ab 1931 ohne Titel Primary Maker: Erwin Heerich 1991 Anatomische Darstellung des Kehlkopfs (Larynx) Primary Maker: Gerhard Halbritter 1960 Zahnschlüssel halbautomatisch Um 1820

8523229287fdf254.text: Expand Download Tapa-Handtasche "siapo" Previous Next Name Haushalt Tapa-Handtasche "siapo" Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 1987 Description Die Handtasche wurde in der Form einer kleinen kreisrunden Trommel hergestellt, die für diese Art des Touristen-Kunsthandwerks sehr beliebt war. In der Technik der Verarbeitung folgt sie völlig dem Stück AOI-Es-A1386; allerdings wurde zwischen die Schicht des Pandanusblatt-Mattengeflechts und den Rindenbaststoff noch eine Schicht Pappe von mittlerer Steifheit geschoben. Die Trageschnur wurde aus naturfarbenem Hibiskusbast als mittelstarker Zopf geflochten. Die Knäufe bestehen aus aufgerollten Pandanusblatt-Streifen. 926bdb5f0d637289.text: Expand Download Images(2) Tapa-Briefmappe "siapo" Previous Next Images(2) Name Haushalt Tapa-Briefmappe "siapo" Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date 1987 Description Die Briefmappe, deren unteres Ende in eine Überlappung am oberen Ende gesteckt und mit einer Lasche an der Rückseite an die Wand gehängt werden kann, wurde aus dem Grundmaterial "siapo" hergestellt: Einem Stück Rindenbaststoff, das eingefärbt und bemalt wurde. Für den Zweck, zu einem "praktischen" Reiseandenken zu werden, wurde es von beiden Seiten mit importierter Klarsichtfolie eingeschlagen, die ringsum mit importierter dünner brauner Baumwollkordel festgenäht wurde. 6b0cf1698b5239a6.title: Malanggan-Schnitzwerk "Totok" – Works – eMuseum
6b0cf1698b5239a6.meta_description: Die Malanggan-Schnitzerei mit Backenbart "totok" besteht aus Holz und hat eine zylindrische Form. Sie wurde in Neuirland hergestellt. Dargestellt ist eine männliche Fi …
6b0cf1698b5239a6.text: Expand Download Malanggan-Schnitzwerk "Totok" Previous Next Name Ritualgegenstand Malanggan-Schnitzwerk "Totok" Department Ethnologische Sammlung Date um 1900 Description Die Malanggan-Schnitzerei mit Backenbart "totok" besteht aus Holz und hat eine zylindrische Form. Sie wurde in Neuirland hergestellt. Dargestellt ist eine männliche Figur, die mit gebeugten Beinen auf einem Sockel steht. Das Geschlecht wird durch den Lendenschurz bedeckt. Die Ohrläppchen sind geschlitzt und überlang. Mit dem Pflock unter dem Sockel wurde es zu der Totengedenkfeier in die Erde gesteckt. Die Tierdarstellungen verweisen unter anderem auf die Clanzugehörigkeit des Verstorbenen. Die Schnitzereien werden speziell für das Fest hergestellt, wobei nicht nur das vollendete Werk und dessen Präsentation, sondern besonders die Herstellung Bedeutung haben. Die hier gezeigte Keule war vermutlich für den Verkauf an Tourist*innen bestimmt. Der für die Stärke des Keulenkopfes viel zu dünne Keulenstab und insbesondere der ornamental ausgeschnitzte Übergang zeigen, dass diese Keule nicht mehr für ihren eigentlichen Zweck zu verwenden wäre. Diese wird heute für die dünnen Linien häufig auch mit einer weichen Schreibfeder, daneben aber auch mit den traditionellen stiftartigen Federn aus der Rippe eines Kokosfiederblattes vorgezeichnet. 2592535f86eb46e6.title: Anatomical Collection - MUT Tübingen
2592535f86eb46e6.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
2592535f86eb46e6.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Human bodies Anatomical Collection The Anatomical Collection is primarily used for medical and dental education. In addition to historical apparatuses, such as the microscope from the Berlin company Bénèche & Wasserlein (1850), it contains a number of anatomical and embryological models and preparations. Wax models reconstructed from microscopic sections are used to illustrate the development of the individual organs in the embryo. Highlights of the collection are the wax model of a primed body from the famous Parisian workshop Tramond (c. 1888), the historical skull collection based on Franz Joseph Gall (1758-1828), and the so-called "Stone Child of Leinzell," a fossilized fetus found in 1720 during the dissection of a 91-year-old woman (currently on loan to the Haus der Geschichte Baden-Württemberg, Stuttgart). To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Phrenological skull according to Franz Josef Gall "Stone child of Leinzell", 1720 Wax model (torso) from the workshop Maison Tramond (Paris, 1888) You can find the unedited version of the pictures by clicking the corresponding titles. Phrenological skull according to Franz Josef Gall (jpg) "Stone child of Leinzell", 1720 Wax model (torso) from the workshop Maison Tramond (Paris, 1888) (jpg) Visit collection Opening hours Mon to Fri from 9am to 5pm Tours Tours currently not available Curator Administration Prof. Dr. Bernhard Hirt Tel: 07071-2973015 send email Dr. Peter Neckel Tel: 07071-2972169 send email Admission Free of charge Address Anatomical Institute (Old Anatomy) Österbergstraße 3 72074 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? 7f872f6cb8910a43.title: Antique Sculptures - MUT Tübingen
7f872f6cb8910a43.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
7f872f6cb8910a43.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Antique Sculptures Collection of mouldings The collection of plaster casts built up by the Institute of Classical Archaeology since the beginning of the nineteenth Century now consists of about 370 objects. The casts of famous statues, most of them life-size, from the largest European museums, as well as reliefs and architectural fragments, offer a fascinating tour of ancient art history from its beginnings to the Roman Imperial period. Among the oldest casts in the collection is one of the Lacocoon group found in Rome in 1506, its marble original is currently in the Vatican. Particularly impressive casts also include the Apollo of Belvedere, the Diana of Versailles, the Discus Thrower of Myron, the Farnese Hercules, and the Nike of Samothrace. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Frieze of the siphnier treasure house in Delphi with a reconstruction proposal of the missing central part. The Laocoon group, plaster cast from Paris, acquired in 1837. After a marble original (?) of the 1st century AD (Rome, Vatican Museums). Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Aegina, warrior head K II, reconstruction proposal Th. Schäfer/S. Alemdar 2015. f1bddfca72bca342.title: Archaeobotanical Collection - MUT Tübingen
f1bddfca72bca342.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
f1bddfca72bca342.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Seeds and fruits Archaeobotanical Collection The Archaeobotanical Collection of the Institute of Natural History Archaeology (INA) has been built up since the 1970s with seeds and fruits from botanical gardens and intensive collecting activities in Europe and the Near East. About 6000 plant species are used mainly by archaeobotanists to identify plant finds from archaeological excavations. These ancient objects are also part of the collection. They include cultivated and wild plant finds from famous Oriental excavations, such as Troia (Turkey) and Qatna (Syria), as well as charcoal from Paleolithic caves in southwestern Germany, all of which provide information about the environment, agriculture and diet of the people of that time. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Oldest spelt remains of emmer wheat, Chogha Golan (Iran), c. 6000 BC. 0370f7b71f1d5ca3.title: Archive of Everyday Cultures - MUT Tübingen
0370f7b71f1d5ca3.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
0370f7b71f1d5ca3.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Archive of Everyday Cultures The “Archive of Everyday Culture” includes various collections of the institute dating back to the 1930s. These include objects of regional everyday life, political insignia, books and mass press, clothing, masks and wall decorations, postcards and advertising pictures, toys and films, photographs and albums, autobiographical texts and files on the history of the field. Since the end of the 1960s, the collection canon of traditional folklore (custom props, house models, traditional costumes, pottery) has been specifically supplemented by products of mass culture.The Arno Ruoff Archive of the Tübingen Center for Language in Southwest Germany is also part of the collection and contains numerous dialect recordings as well as a digital language atlas. The archive is used for teaching and research, it lends objects to exhibitions and is open to external scholars by appointment. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). 444c56c48283048e.title: Arno-Ruoff-Archive - MUT Tübingen
444c56c48283048e.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
444c56c48283048e.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Conversations Arno-Ruoff-Archive The “Arno Ruoff Archive” consists of more than 2000 audio recordings that the linguist Arno Ruoff (1930–2010) has made in the Southwest since the 1950s, initially together with the cultural scientist Hermann Bausinger (1926–2021). The recordings stored in the archive are unrehearsed free conversations, most of whose audio carriers fell victim to a fire in 2016. However, all of the recordings are available digitally and a large portion has already been transcribed. Today, the documents provide an insight into the everyday life of people in Baden-Württemberg, Bavarian Swabia, Vorarlberg, and Liechtenstein from around 1880 to 2000. The archive is used for teaching and research. On request, individual recordings can be listened to and the transcriptions can be viewed for research work. More detailed information on the recording locations can be found on the website of the “Tübinger Arbeitsstelle Sprache in Südwestdeutschland” of the Ludwig Uhland Institute. Highlights of the Collection Typewriter and tape by Arno-Ruoff. fde24ebd2cdab968.title: Astrophysical Collection - MUT Tübingen
fde24ebd2cdab968.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
fde24ebd2cdab968.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Space Astrophysical Collection The collection of the Institute of Astronomy and Astrophysics, founded in 1964 as Chair of Theoretical Astrophysics, includes the first cast-iron quadrant acquired by Cassini in 1752 for the observatory of Hohentübingen Castle. Other instruments in the collection include a portable passage instrument and a parallactic-mounted telescope, acquired a hundred years later, which made it possible to take much more precise measurements. Instruments built at the institute, beginning in the mid-1960s, bear witness to more recent scientific activities. These unique instruments, including an Aries space mirror, were developed, built, and used for space-based telescopes. They could be used to observe radiation from cosmic objects that cannot be measured from the ground. The only space telescope that has left Earth twice and returned safely is the highlight of the collection. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). 35a4931be5e5d4b2.title: August Halm Archive - MUT Tübingen
35a4931be5e5d4b2.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
35a4931be5e5d4b2.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] August Halm Archive August Halm Archive As a scholar, music writer, teacher, and composer, August Halm (1869–1929) influenced the musical life in Württemberg, Bavaria, and the Free School Community of Wickersdorf in Thuringia. During his studies, Halm spent time in Munich with Josef Rheinberger and in Tübingen during the tenure of the University Music Director Emil Kauffmann. As a composer, he was initially influenced by Anton Bruckner and Hugo Wolf. Later, he adopted forms and techniques from the Baroque era. The Halm Archive at the Institute of Musicology, University of Tübingen, contains manuscripts and prints, as well as letters, books, recordings of individual works, and files from the former August Halm society. Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Two card boxes with the name ‘August Halm’, including contact persons Magnetophone tapes BASF type LGS Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Three books on display, written by August Halm, ‘Von zwei Kulturen der Musik’ and two editions of ‘Harmonielehre’ from the Göschen collection The collections of the Institute of Musicology include also the Musical Instrument Collection , Record Collection of the Musicology Department , Swabian State Music Archive , and Portrait Collection Goldberg . headings: The biblical life in everyday life
b38295ace65254de.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] The biblical life in everyday life Biblical-Archaeological Collection The collection of the Biblical-Archaeological Institute was established in 1960 when the institute was founded. It includes about 430 objects, mainly ancient ceramic vessels (picture), besides coins, seals, and models. The objects range from the Early Bronze Age (from 3500 BC) to Late Antiquity. Several showcases present the thematically arranged objects. Besides offering insights into the life of the Israelites of the 1st millennium B.C., the development of oil lamps is illustrated by way of example.Only a small part of the collection is open to the public. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). ade81405a3c33015.title: Biochemical Instrument Collection - MUT Tübingen
ade81405a3c33015.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
ade81405a3c33015.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Biochemical Instrument Collection The Biochemical Instrument Collection houses historical laboratory equipment from the former “physiological-chemical institute” (since 1885/86). Among the preserved objects from the time of the “Schlosslabor” since 1816, the world's oldest biochemical laboratory, are an iron retort, several digestion crucibles, and a beam scale.Special instruments for hemoglobin research, for example, date from the time of the physiological-chemical institute in Gmelinstrasse (1886–1964). Most of the collection is housed in today's Interfaculty Institute of Biochemistry (IFIB); a selection of remarkable exhibits can be seen in the permanent exhibition “Tübingen Castle Laboratory – Cradle of Biochemistry” at Hohentübingen Castle. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). 2502cb7c9acd2b06.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Compounds Biochemical Preparation Collection The preparation collection of the former Tübingen “physiological-chemical institute” is located at today's Interfaculty Institute for Biochemistry of the University of Tübingen IFIB. Part of it can be seen in the permanent exhibition “Tübingen Castle Laboratory – Cradle of Biochemistry” at Hohentübingen Castle. Most of the objects in the collection date from the period between 1886 and 1964, when the institute was housed in Gmelinstraße. Among the more than 30 specimens that were created in the so-called “Schlosslabor” before 1886 is a test tube inscribed – in his own hand – by the physician and physiologist Friedrich Miescher (1844–1895), the discoverer of nucleic acid, the substance of DNA and RNA. It contains nuclein from salmon sperm and was made around 1871. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). 4c875b97a15f5040.title: Botanical Gardens with Collections - MUT Tübingen
4c875b97a15f5040.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
4c875b97a15f5040.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Botanical Gardens with Collections Among the collections, the Botanical Garden has a special position, as living “objects” can be seen here. On ten hectares one can find almost 10,000 plant species from all over the world. The plants are arranged according to geographic, ecological, and relationship aspects.Rhododendrons are a focal point, with about 75 cultivated forms and over 170 wild species. The systematic section – below the Tropicarium – shows the families of flowering plants based on their relationships. Above the North Ring, the Arboretum, a systematically arranged collection of woody plants, is located on five hectares. The greenhouses present the subtropical and tropical plant world on more than 3000 square meters. The Botanical Garden sees itself as a place of education as well as a meeting and recreation place for the urban population. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). 4370bff606f29c19.title: Castle Barrel - MUT Tübingen
4370bff606f29c19.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
4370bff606f29c19.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Castle Barrel Beneath the large Knights' Hall of the Museum of Ancient Cultures, in the cellar of Hohentübingen Castle, is the oldest preserved giant wine barrel in the world. In 1546, Duke Ulrich of Württemberg (1487–1550) had this barrel built by the Bönnigheim cooper Simon Binder. The volume of the barrel is about 84,000 liters. Today, the barrel holds an entry in the Guinness Book of Records due to its age. Since 1991, the oldest giant barrel was no longer open to visitors because a colony of mouse-eared bats lives in the rear area of the castle cellar. In 2018, however, access to this superlative was granted again under strict precautions, including that guided tours are only offered during the winter months. Get additional information on the Museum | Ancient Cultures website and tickets at www.fassticket.de . a922778b049e484e.title: Castle Church - MUT Tübingen
a922778b049e484e.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
a922778b049e484e.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Castle Church Today’s castle church belongs to the type “hall church” and its structural equipment, such as the wall painting, pews, and ceiling, goes back to the time of the Nordic Neo-Renaissance of the nineteenth century. During the great castle reconstructions during the sixteenth century under Duke Ulrich of Württemberg (1487–1550), the castle church was moved to its present location in the south wing in 1535. Thus, it is the oldest Protestant castle church of all. During the French siege of the castle at the end of the Thirty Years' War, it was severely damaged in 1647, making extensive renovation necessary. Since the handover of the entire castle to the university in 1816 by King Wilhelm I of Württemberg (1781–1864), the castle church together with the sacristy has served as the seat of the Protestant Preaching Institute. Here, theology students have received part of their practical theological training for over 200 years. The Castle Church houses the Collection of the Protestant Preachers Institute . d205ca4fc7e521fe.title: Castle Laboratory - MUT Tübingen
d205ca4fc7e521fe.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
d205ca4fc7e521fe.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Castle Laboratory The former kitchen at Hohentübingen Castle was transformed by the university into the world's first biochemical laboratory in the early nineteenth century. Today, this room is used as a museum, a place of scientific history. It was here that the first German biochemist Georg Karl Ludwig Sigwart (1784–1864) worked from 1818; it was here that Felix Hoppe-Seyler (1825–1895) researched and named hemoglobin, and it was here that Friedrich Miescher (1844–1895) discovered nucleic acid, the basic substance of the hereditary substances DNA and RNA, in 1869. Thanks to the financial support of the biopharmaceutical company CureVac AG, the university set up a permanent interactive exhibition at the historic site in 2015. At the center of the presentation is Miescher's still-existing test tube with nucleic acid and other historical laboratory equipment and preparations, which help to present the decisive chapters of biochemistry. 11a66575f6bae05c.title: Castle Observatory - MUT Tübingen
11a66575f6bae05c.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
11a66575f6bae05c.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Castle Observatory The small but historically important observatory at Hohentübingen Castle was built in 1814 specifically to use the so-called “Reichenbach’ Repetitive Circle”. Only a few of these precision devices repetitive circles can be found worldwide, for example in Florence, Milan and Paris. However, the unique ensemble of this historical device and specially built observatory exists only in Tübingen. The Tübingen professor of mathematics Johann G. F. Bohnenberger (1765–1831) is closely associated with this building. As an astronomer, physicist, and surveyor, he was also responsible for pioneering developments and the invention of the gyroscope. To mark the 200 th anniversary of the Württemberg land survey, which originated at this site, at the northeast tower of the castle, the observatory was renovated in 2018 and made accessible to visitors. d2ed031359acbd9d.title: Ceramics Collection of Medieval Archaeology - MUT Tübingen
d2ed031359acbd9d.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
d2ed031359acbd9d.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Ceramics Collection of Medieval Archaeology The comparison and analysis of ceramics are an important criterion for the chronological, geographical, and social classification of past cultures. Numerous unlocated sherds from the collection of Albert Kley (1907–2001) and not accurately assignable rejects from the then State Monuments Office in Tübingen served as the basis of the ceramics collection, which was established by the Department of Medieval Archaeology. In the winter semester 2008/09 the collection was organized in the context of a lecture course and partly made accessible via the internet. The collection offers a good overview of the development of ceramic objects in Southwest Germany from the Migration Period to the Early Modern Period. Highlights of the Collection Several ceramic shards from the collection Shard of a pot typical for the "Karniesrand" (ogee rim) style of the late Middle Ages Photo: MUT Specialized potteries began to emerge in the Middle Ages. headings: Chart Collection of the ENT Clinic
4fade368d4c01de9.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Chart Collection of the ENT Clinic The chart collection of the Ear, Nose and Throat (ENT) Clinic in Tübingen comprises around 120 individual items. The majority of these panels, some of which are over 100 years old, date from the 1960s. Most of them are one-of-a-kind pieces that were manually produced by artists in collaboration with physicians and signed and dated at the end of the design process in a manner comparable to paintings. Among the scientific illustrators and graphic artists who created the boards of the ENT clinic are names such as Gerhard Halbritter (1908–2002), Karl-Heinz Seeber (1924–2005) and the Tübingen artist Theodor Baumann (1855–1932). To the collection in the eMuseum . Highlights of the Collection © MUT | V. b196054b2af4ca51.title: Coin Collection of Classical Archaeology - MUT Tübingen
b196054b2af4ca51.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
b196054b2af4ca51.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Coin Collection of Classical Archaeology The coin collection, whose roots go back to 1798, is today one of the largest and most important university collections of its kind in Germany, with approximately 20,000 objects. The main focus is on about 8000 ancient Greek and 6000 Roman coins, in addition to 2000 medieval and early modern coins as well as 3000 medals. Particularly beautiful pieces are an archaic silver coin from Kaulonia in Lower Italy or the famous Athenian tetradrachms from the middle of the fifth century BC.Combined with the coin collection of Islamic Numismatics, the coin collection of the University of Tübingen is the most extensive of any university. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT Landau siege cliff of Duke Karl Alexander (Tüb. Inv. e01de05853ef50a7.title: Coin Collection of Islamic Numismatics - MUT Tübingen
e01de05853ef50a7.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
e01de05853ef50a7.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Coin Collection of Islamic Numismatics The founder of Swabian folklore Ernst Meier (1813–1866), Tübingen professor of oriental languages, collected Arabic coins, which were purchased by the university after his death. Through the acquisition of the Stephen Album collection in 1988, numerous donations and the founding of the “Research Center for Islamic Numismatics Tübingen” (FINT), this basic stock became one of the world's largest and most important collections of Islamic coins of the Middle Ages and early modern period. Around 80,000 coinages, more than 3,000 of them in gold (picture), from the area from Spain to Afghanistan offer an archive of the history of rulers, religion, and economy. Together with the coin collection of Classical Archaeology (see p. 20), the coin collection of the University of Tübingen forms the most qualitative and extensive university coin collection in Germany. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Counterstamps on silver coins allow tracing of circulation routes and duration, Central Asia, 15th century Moroccan gold coins (1370-1420), discovered in the 1940s in Butera (Sicily, Italy). d13a5cd963ba571a.title: Collection for Ethics and History of Medicine - MUT Tübingen
d13a5cd963ba571a.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
headings: Collection for Ethics and History of Medicine
d13a5cd963ba571a.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection for Ethics and History of Medicine Im Jahre 1961 wurde das Institut für Geschichte der Medizin eingerichtet. In der Folge vereinigte man das Institut mit dem Lehrstuhl für Ethik in der Medizin zum Institut für Ethik und Geschichte der Medizin. Es erhielt vielfältige Schenkungen von Tübinger Professoren, Kliniken und Instituten, darunter eine verstörende Sammlung, die Hans Fleischhacker (1912 –1992) im Jahr 1943 für seine Tübinger Habilitationsschrift heranzog, besteht aus mehr als 600 Hand-, Fuß- und Fingerabdrücken überwiegend jüdischer Insassen des Ghettos Litzmannstadt – nahe Łódź, Polen. Der damalige Rassenbiologe und SS-Obersturmführer Fleischhacker wollte anhand dieser Bestände vermeintlich morphologische Unterschiede zwischen den Handflächen von Juden und Nichtjuden belegen. Es ist die einzige Sammlung ihrer Art. Durch sie erhalten wir heute authentische Einblicke in das bizarre Zusammenspiel von Ideologie und Wissenschaft während der Zeit des Nationalsozialismus. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Stereoscopic Atlas of Neuroanatomy. The individual maps inserted into a stereoscope enabled the viewer to gain spatial and plastic impressions of the human brain Stereoscope made by Keystone View Company, 1947. The stereoscope, made of wood and glass lenses, allows two-dimensional images to be perceived as three-dimensional objects Trepanation set, 1841. The drills contained were used by doctors to open skulls Visit collection Opening hours By appointment Tours Tours currently not available Curator Collection management PD Dr. Henning Tümmers Tel: 07071 2975216 Send email Admission Free of charge Address Collection for Ethics and History of Medicine | Section History of Medicine Goethestraße 6 72076 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? 82757b8744cbcf83.title: Collection Halbritter - MUT Tübingen
82757b8744cbcf83.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
82757b8744cbcf83.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection Halbritter In 2009, the MUT acquired clay models and casting molds from the estate of Gerhard Richard Halbritter (1908–2002), a sculptor, draftsman, and graphic artist from Tübingen who was born in Mühlhausen/Thuringia. These are a selection of designs or models of portrait reliefs (picture) of representatives of the University of Tübingen. The collection also includes original negatives of faculty plaques and university medals by the artist's hand. Halbritter became known primarily for the death masks he made of Ernst Bloch or the members of the Red Army Faction (RAF) Gudrun Ensslin, Andreas Baader and Jan-Carl Raspe. 06c870408aeb1de5.title: Collection Micropaleontology - MUT Tübingen
06c870408aeb1de5.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
06c870408aeb1de5.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection Micropaleontology An extensive collection on micropaleontology was established in the Department of Geosciences. It is assigned to the Paleontological Collection. Especially sediment samples collected by drilling contain fossil microorganisms. Such often unicellular microorganisms are of great importance for stratigraphic and taxonomic research and for environmental and climatic questions of geological history. Since the 1950s, renowned micropaleontologists, such as Alfred Eisenack (1891–1982) and Hans Gocht (1930–2014), conducted research and taught in Tübingen. In 1976, the collection already included 11,500 specimens. Since 1983, the institute has published the monographic series “Tübingen Micropaleontological Notices”. Currently, several scientists from Tübingen are working in this field. 14511c477a34e19c.title: Collection of Ancient Oriental Studies - MUT Tübingen
14511c477a34e19c.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
14511c477a34e19c.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Cuneiforms Collection of Ancient Oriental Studies The collection of Mesopotamic cuneiform texts, which was only founded after World War II, covers the entire range of Ancient Near Eastern script development from the third to the first millenium BCE. Over this long period of time, the gradual changes in the characters can be traced. The texts allow an insight into different types of ancient Near Eastern writing, such as charters, letters, lexical texts, and royal inscriptions. Objects of small art, such as original seals and replicas of ancient oriental sculptures and relief art, complete the collection. A medal commemorates Georg Friedrich Grotefend (1775-1853), one of the early pioneers of Ancient Near Eastern studies and the first decipherer of the cuneiform script. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection This letter in which the businessman Niginakia and his associate named Qishti-Erra are mentioned dates from ancient Babylonian times (17th century BC) Fragment of an originally two-column inscription by the king Siniddinam of Larsa. King Siniddinam (1777–1771 BC) is also mentioned in the inscription. Fragment of an official document from the age of Uruk III (around 3000 BC) Visit museum Opening hours Wed to Sun from 10am to 5pm Thurs from 10am to 7pm Tours “Sundays at 3” all year round Group and school tours by appointment Book tour Tel: 07071-2977579 Curator Administration Prof. Dr. Wiebke Meinhold Tel: 07071 - 2976758 Send email Prof. Dr. Andreas Fuchs Tel: 07071 - 2977148 Send email Admission Adults: 5€ Reduced: 3€ (children, pupils, students, pensioners, severely disabled people) Family ticket: 12€ Students of Tübingen: Free of charge Address Collection of Ancient Oriental Studies | Museum Ancient Cultures Burgsteige 11 | Hohentübingen Castle 72070 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? 517a30def5975417.title: Sammlung der Anorganischen Chemie - MUT Tübingen
517a30def5975417.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
517a30def5975417.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Sammlung der Anorganischen Chemie The collection of experimental preparation of inorganic chemistry consists mostly of objects used by Wilhelm Johann Schlenk (1879–1943). Schlenk was a German chemist who taught at the University of Tübingen from 1935. The objects kept in the collection are strongly influenced by the so-called “Schlenk technique” developed by Schlenk. It characterizes a chemical working method with which substances sensitive to air or moisture can be processed under exclusion of atmospheric oxygen and humidity. Some old Schlenk vessels containing substances were found during remodeling work. In addition, the experimental preparation of inorganic chemistry has an extensive collection of chemicals stored in historical glass bottles. headings: Collection of Paintings of the University
c552f7c1a50a6018.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection of Paintings of the University This collection includes works by old masters as well as contemporary paintings. The latter are also purchased, for instance, as part of the “Invited Artist” project. Among the special works, for example, is the large, impressive painting by the Offenburg astronomical photographer and painter Julius Grimm (1842–1906) from 1895: The “View of the Full Moon” (image). The oil painting currently hangs in the Knights' Hall of Hohentübingen Castle and is open to the public. In addition to its size, it is particularly impressive for its almost photorealistic painterly quality. Grimm produced his view of the moon with the help of three-dimensional models based on his own photographs. How the large version in Tübingen came to the university is unclear. What is known is that Tübingen geologist Wolf von Engelhardt, alerted by the caretaker, found the painting in the attic of the Neue Aula in 1986. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Julius Grimm: View of the Full Moon, 1895, oil/canvas, 220 x 220 cm Board of shame at the Hohentübingen Castle, 1519 (CC, Wikipedia) The moon painting is currently hanging in the Knights Hall of the Museum of Ancient Cultures Visit collection Opening hours By appointment Tours Tours are not available Curator Contact Dr. Ariane Koller Tel: 07071-2977058 Send email Admission Free of charge Address At various places of the university c/o Graphical Collection| Bonatz building Wilhelmstraße 32 72074 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? c3c50b7d511ddfa4.title: Collection of Prehistory - MUT Tübingen
c3c50b7d511ddfa4.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
c3c50b7d511ddfa4.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection of Prehistory The Collection of Prehistory contains significant finds from the beginning of human history. One focus is on objects from the Palaeolithic Age, which were found in caves in the Swabian Alb. Excavations carried out in 1931 under Gustav Riek (1900-1976) have yielded several ivory figurines from 40,000-year-old strata, which rank among the oldest figurative works of human art. They provide the earliest evidence of essential human cultural techniques worldwide – for art, music, and religion.A second focus is on the finds made by Ludwig Kohl-Larsen in the 1930s in the Mumba Cave in what is now Tanzania. Part of these assemblages are also important hominin remains, such as the upper jaw of an Australopithecus afarensis (Garusi) and the world's oldest human remains of a Homo sapiens (Eyasi). To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). b7d7713a232ef388.title: Collection of Recent Prehistory - MUT Tübingen
b7d7713a232ef388.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. b7d7713a232ef388.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection of Recent Prehistory The teaching collection of the Institute of Early History goes back to the prehistorian and Tübingen professor Robert Rudolf Schmidt (1882–1950), who founded the Institute of Early History in 1921.The education of students was at the core of the development of the collection objects. In addition, the collection also houses some assemblages of supraregional importance. These are mainly finds from excavations of the Neolithic and Late Bronze Age wetland settlements at the Federsee, which have been part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site “Prehistoric Lake Dwellings around the Alps” since 2011. Also on display are objects from the Iron Age fortified settlements of the Heuneburg on the upper Danube and Heidengraben near Grabenstetten on the Swabian Alb. A large number of objects can also be found in the eMuseum of the MUT. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT Copies of golden bowls from the Bronze Age hoard find of Eberswalde (county Barnim). The originals are now located in the State Museum for Fine Arts A.S. headings: Collection of the Protestant Preachers Institute
cbd42c5d3429e0c7.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection of the Protestant Preachers Institute The Preachers Institute of the Protestant Theology Faculty has maintained its seat in the castle church in Hohentübingen Castle since its foundation in 1815. Theology professor Jonathan Friedrich Bahnmaier (1774–1841) was the first to prepare theology candidates for their preaching, worship and teaching practice in the chapel and sacristy in a novel seminaristic-conservatory style. At the interface of university, church, and society, the “spiritual polyclinic” was meant to provide prospective preachers and teachers of religion with a comprehensive practical theological education. To this day, courses of the Institute of Practical Theology are taught there. The collection of the Protestant Preachers Institute is simultaneously the church furnishings. It consists for instance of paintings of the eighteenth century and liturgical equipment used in worships. fbf4b847a7f5bbd2.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collection Women's Clinic The collection of the University Women's Clinic dates back to the time of Johann Heinrich Ferdinand Autenrieth (1772–1835). He is considered to be the founder of the University Hospital and was an important initiator of women's and obstetrics medicine in Württemberg in the nineteenth century. The collection contains various wet preparations, diverse models and instruments, the majority of which date from the twentieth century. Among the special pieces is a wooden birthing chair with embossed leather upholstery from the beginning of the nineteenth century, which is on permanent loan to the Haus der Geschichte Baden-Württemberg in Stuttgart. edc86e08ad978a99.title: Collections Lessing and Schmalzriedt - MUT Tübingen
edc86e08ad978a99.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
edc86e08ad978a99.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collections Lessing and Schmalzriedt The Schmalzriedt Collection includes 95 Turkmen rugs from the nineteenth and early twentieth century. They come from the Turkmen tribes of the Yomud and Tekke. In addition to rugs, the collection includes bags, horse blankets, tent entrances, and long tent borders. The woven fabrics are of particular interest because of their ornamentation, which helps to locate the individual pieces very precisely, both regionally and historically. The private collection of Professor Egidius Schmalzriedt (1935–2003) was donated to the university in 2010 from his estate. A second private collection was donated to the university in 2018 by the couple Gotthold Ephraim and Johanna Lessing. It contains about 40 Turkmen carpets, the majority of which were acquired in the 1970s and 1980s. The textiles were probably produced in the nineteenth century. Highlights of the Collection Large tent bag (choval), Yomut Hearth rug (Odschak basch), Yomut Turkmen weaving Sammlungen Schmalzriedt und Lessing Visit collection Opening hours Currently not open to the public Tours Tours currently not available Curator Contact Dr. Edgar Bierende Tel: 07071-2977481 Send email Dr. Lutz Ilisch Send email Address Museum of the University of Tübingen MUT | Depot Sand 5 72076 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? 0849147114aa1aad.title: Collections of the University Archives - MUT Tübingen
0849147114aa1aad.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. 0849147114aa1aad.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collections of the University Archives The University Archive not only preserves archival records of Tübingen's university history. It also owns supplementary collections of very different kinds, such as pamphlets from the 1968 student movement or studentica from the nineteenth century. In addition, this collection also includes graphics, silhouettes, and large historical photo collections from various disciplines and institutes. A collection of audio tapes of university radio broadcasts (1988–2016) has also been archived. Museum objects range from early modern money chests to typars. Such stamps, especially university and faculty seals, are among the pieces of special rank, as they were used to authenticate important documents. Each month, an interesting object from the of the University Library's or University Archive's collection is presented under the headin " Object of the month ". Highlights of the Collection Deed of Abbot Heinrich Fabri of Blaubeuren about the foundation of the university, March 11, 1477, Template UAT U1 Studentica: 1972 pamphlet and pipe bowl, 19th c. Family album of Friedrich Christoph Mayer, 1780 The holdings of the University Archive include the Collection Schmidgall . 85e3de8dbcea8dd9.title: Collections of the University Library - MUT Tübingen
85e3de8dbcea8dd9.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
85e3de8dbcea8dd9.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Collections of the University Library The University Library has been acquiring literature from all disciplines since its foundation in the late fifteenth century and currently has a collection of about four million items. Of great value are the historical holdings, such as original manuscripts from India (almost 900) and the Near East (over 900), about 350 occidental medieval manuscripts and 3000 manuscripts from the early modern period, 2200 incunabula and almost 12,000 prints from the sixteenth century. In total, the Rara holdings include about 90,000 volumes, mainly books printed before 1700, first editions or other precious works. The historical holdings also include bequests, manuscripts, autographs, sheet music, portraits, and cadastral maps, as well as an extensive record collection and a cannonball from the siege of Hohentübingen Castle in the seventeenth century. Many of the UB's historical holdings have already been digitized and are freely accessible. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Each month, an interesting object from the of the University Library's or University Archive's collection is presented under the headin " Object of the month ". Highlights of the Collection Atharvaveda, Paippalādaśākhā (Śāradā script, birch bark, Kashmir, 16th century, Ma I 421). Kufic Quranic fragment, Sura 17:37-36:57 (649-675, Ma VI 165). Armenian Gospels (Constantinople, 1644, Ma XIII 4) Visit collection Opening hours Mon to Fri from 9am to 12pm and from 1pm to 4pm Tours Tours currently not available Curator Administration Kristina Stöbener M.A. Tel: 07071-2972834 Send email Admission Free of charge Address University Library of Tübingen | Department of Manuscripts and Historical Prints Wilhelmstraße 32 72074 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? 5b35e17664a147b5.title: Computer Collection - MUT Tübingen
5b35e17664a147b5.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
5b35e17664a147b5.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Computer Collection The Wilhelm Schickard Institute displays a replica of a calculating machine by Tübingen professor Wilhelm Schickard (1592–1635), which is considered the first of its kind worldwide and is regarded as the prelude to computer technology. The computer collection focuses on the documentation of two revolutions in computer technology: In 1957, Kenneth Olsen and Harlan Anderson founded the company DEC (Digital Equipment Corporation) with the aim of producing small computers that were suitable for offices and laboratories without their own operating personnel and without air conditioning. In 1960, they introduced their PDP 1 (Programmed Data Processor) to the market and revolutionized the idea of computers. Instead of huge machines costing millions of dollars, the PDPs created a sensation. Soon, a new market for hobbyist computers and personal computers (PCs) developed. The collection shows some devices from this PC world. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Commodore PET 2001 (Personal Electronic Transactor), 1977 Replica of the calculating machine of Wilhelm Schickhard from 1623 Visit collection Opening hours Mon to Fri, 8am to 8pm Tours Tours currently not available Curator Contact Prof. Dr. Oliver Bringmann Phone: 07071-2970451 send e-mail Admission Free of charge Address Computer Collection | Wilhelm-Schickard-Institute Sand 1, 13, 14 (2. 283a57cb797789e0.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
283a57cb797789e0.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Dental Collection The teaching collection of the University Clinic for Dentistry, Oral Medicine and Maxillofacial Surgery (UKZMK) is located on the first floor of the clinic. The chair of dentistry has existed in Tübingen since 1910. The first director Hermann Peckert (1876–1940) already founded the collection.In an award-winning project called DENTAL |THINGS between the MUT and UKZMK, the collection was transformed into a museum. Objects from all fields of dentistry, including research and teaching, are presented as examples. Display boards explain the general history of dentistry in general and specific to Tübingen in relation to the exhibits, which range from the sixteenth century to the present. An online exhibition on DENTAL |THINGS can be found here. To the collection at the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Saint Apollonia with her attribute of pliers, patron saint of dentists, plaster, early 20th c. Obturator prosthesis with palate for defect closure (made of rubber) Pelican (tooth crusher) for tooth extraction, with rotary knob, early modern period Visit collection Opening hours Mon to Fri from 8am to 5pm Tours By appointment Curator Collection management Dr. Andreas Prutscher ZA Tel: 07071-2983461 Send email Admission Free of charge Address Dental Collection | Center for Dental, Oral and Maxillofacial Medicine Osianderstraße 2–8 72076 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? ed2673131fcb29a7.title: Drill Core Collection - MUT Tübingen
ed2673131fcb29a7.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
ed2673131fcb29a7.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Drill Core Collection The newly constructed building of the Geo and Environmental Center (GUZ) at Morgenstelle, built between 2015 and 2018, is home to a high-quality repository. Parts of the Mineralogical Collection as well as a collection of drilling and sediment cores are stored in it. They are kept on a floor space of about 250 m². In Tübingen, geology research has been carried out with the help of drill cores for many decades. As early as 1920, a 92.9-meter-long drill core from the Lengetal II borehole near Geislingen passed from the Staatliches Tiefbohramt to the Tübingen collection (Fossil index, catalog no.: 18822). The core collection today comprises cores of about 3.5 kilometers in length; of these, more than two-thirds are marine cores. 9231900e3b882175.title: Egyptian Collection - MUT Tübingen
9231900e3b882175.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
headings: One of the most important Egyptological university collections
9231900e3b882175.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Gods and graves Egyptian Collection The Egyptian Collection covers the period from Egyptian prehistory around 5500 BC to the Christian era in the seventh century AD. Displayed are mainly everyday objects and artifacts of the belief in the afterlife. Including ceramic and stone vessels, bronze votive offerings as well as burial objects and grave goods, the collection shows an impressive spectrum of Egyptian culture, whose relics offer a profound insight into pharaonic Egypt. Among the outstanding highlights are the offering chamber of Seschemnefer III and his wive Hetepheres from Giza and the coffin of Idi from Asyut, its lid equipped with a sidereal clock that is only rarely preserved. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt The sacrificial chamber of Seschemnefer III from Giza is one of the scientific and popular highlights at the same time. Painted limestone, approx. 2380 BCE. Coffin of Idi from Asyut, the lid of which is decorated with a seldom surviving sidereal clock. Plaster on wood, approx. 1900 BCE. Photo: MUT One of the most important Egyptological university collections The foundation of the collection was laid at the beginning of the 20th century, mainly through numerous donations by Ernst von Sieglin. Sieglin's donations are still the main attraction of the collection today and have made it known beyond Germany. After the foundation of the Egyptological Institute in 1959, it was mainly thanks to the institute's founder Hellmut Brunner and his wife Emma Brunner-Traut that the collection could be steadily expanded. In addition to purchases and donations, the ancient Egyptian holdings of the Lindenmuseum in Stuttgart were acquired; numerous other objects are on permanent loan from the Landesmuseum Württemberg in Stuttgart. Conceived primarily as a teaching collection for students and a research facility for specialists, the collection provides a complete overview of life in ancient Egypt. However, due to the exceptional quality of many of the exhibits, it also offers interested laypersons an excellent opportunity to familiarize themselves with the culture of Pharaonic Egypt. Visit museum Opening hours Wed to Sun from 10am to 5pm Thurs from 10am to 7pm Tours “Sundays at 3” all year round Group and school tours by appointment Book tour Tel: 07071-2977579 Curator Custodian Dr. Carolina Teotino-Tattko Tel: 07071-2976061 Send email Admission Adults: 5€ Reduced: 3€ (children, pupils, students, pensioners, severely disabled people) Family ticket: 12€ Students of Tübingen: Free of charge Address Egyptian Collection | Museum Ancient Cultures Burgsteige 11 | Hohentübingen Castle 72070 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? b9a851359fb23004.title: Ethnological Collection - MUT Tübingen
b9a851359fb23004.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
b9a851359fb23004.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] World Cultures Ethnological Collection The collection, founded between 1890 and 1910, now belongs to the Ethnological Department of the Institute of Asian and Oriental Studies. It comprises about 4,000 ethnographica, about 2,500 glass plate slides, as well as 250 watercolors and drawings. The permanent exhibition in the Fünfeckturm (pentagonal tower) of the castle shows a selection that reflects the regional focus of the collection – the Pacific and Amazon. Highlights include Shipibo-Conibo clay vessels from Peru and Malanggan carvings from the Southwest Pacific. An outstanding piece in the collection is a wall panel - known as the "Poupou" - being the ancestral representation of Hinematioro, a Maori princess. It is said that the panel was given to the delegation on James Cook's first voyage to the South Seas (1768-1771) by the Maori. For conservation purposes, it is exhibited only on special occasions. Important objects of the collection are documented in the eMuseum of the MUT. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). Highlights of the Collection Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt During James Cook’s first South Sea trip, this Maori carving was gifted to the expedition participant, Joseph Banks. The native name of the work is poupou. It was produced out of totara wood and is dyed red (New Zealand, mid 18th century) “Tatanua“ mask from the Malanggan complex of the island New Ireland (Papua New Guinea) Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Photo: MUT | Valentin Marquardt Malanggan figures in the Tübingen exhibition (New Ireland, Papua New Guinea) History of the collection The founder of the collection and the institute was Augustin Krämer (1865–1941). Much of the South Sea collections can be traced back to him and his wife Elisabeth Krämer-Bannow (1874–1945), who together undertook five extensive expeditions to the region. About 1,700 of the 4,000 objects in the collection come from the South Sea Islands. A very large collection on the culture of an native society living in the upper reaches of the Amazon was established in 1983 by two postgraduate students from the institute in Tübingen. Overall, however, the teaching collection contains artefacts of diverse origins. A first convolute of objects from Africa, America and Asia had already been collected by the Geographical Institute of the University at the beginning of the 20th century. However, the collection was not systematised until Augustin Krämer founded the Institute of Ethnology as a department of the Institute of Geography in 1919. His private collection of around 1,000 ethnographic objects from Oceania formed the basis of its collection. Krämer continued to expand the collection until his retirement in 1933. Dr Elisabeth Gerdts-Rupp (1888–1972) was in charge of the collection until the late 1950s; she ensured that ethnology continued to be taught in Tübingen in the post-war period, but was unable to prevent the collection from stagnating. It was not until the early 1980s that the position of curator of the collection was created at the already independent institute. Dr Volker Harms took up the post. Under his curation, the collection's American focus has been expanded by some 500 objects, while some 250 objects from Oceania have been added, acquired and documented by members of the Institute during fieldwork. Building on the existing collection, Dr Harms established the permanent ethnological exhibition in 1998 as a department of the Museum of the University of Tübingen at Hohentübingen Castle. Visit museum Opening hours Thu 2 to 6 pm (with consultation hours for the Ethnological Collection Tübingen) Tours Group and school tours by appointment Book tour Curator Administration Dr. Markus Schleiter Tel: 07071-2978537 Send email Admission Adults: 5€ Reduced: 3€ (children, pupils, students, pensioners, severely disabled people) Family ticket: 12€ Students of Tübingen: Free of charge Address WeltKulturen | Pentagonal Tower Burgsteige 11 | Hohentübingen Castle 72070 Tübingen Zum Seitenanfang scrollen What are you looking for? fb1cb1ec984269fa.title: Folded Book Collection Heidtmann - MUT Tübingen
fb1cb1ec984269fa.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
fb1cb1ec984269fa.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Folded Book Collection Heidtmann The Folded Book Collection Heidtmann of the Museum of the University of Tübingen consists of a few specimens from the field of Folded Book Art. In this special field of book art, the books themselves become autonomous aesthetic objects with no practical use by artfully altering their pages through folding. In most cases, these folds relate to the contents of the books, which become sculptures in their own right. This creates exciting interactions between former function and new form. As a passionate artist, the retired Berlin library scientist Prof. Dr. Frank Heidtmann (*1937) transformed his research object into book art in the narrowest sense of the word. Folding utility books such as catalogs, encyclopedias, thick commentaries, and novels into book objects results in a completely different view of the medium book, which now presents itself in a new aesthetic appearance. To the collection in the eMuseum . Highlights of the Collection Buchfalt-Sammlung Heidtmann © MUT Tübingen Cylindrical book sculpture without title, folded from "The best Office 2000 secrets" by Johann-Christian Hanke. This paper sculpture was folded into a spinning top shape with the top and bottom flattened. The novel "The Secret Cross" by Tom Harper was used by the artist. 7d282613be3b4e70.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. headings: Gärtner Collection at the Herbarium Tubingense
7d282613be3b4e70.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Gärtner Collection at the Herbarium Tubingense The “Gärtner Collection” comprises two scientific bequests. It was donated to the university by Emma Gärtner (1818–1875), daughter of the botanist Carl Friedrich Gärtner (1772–1850), on January 9, 1860. It contains a carpological collection of about 600 fruits and seeds, mostly preserved in coverslips, the majority of which are by Joseph Gärtner (1732–1791). Among them are quite a few type specimens that still serve as irreplaceable evidence for international research. Another part of the collection goes back to Carl Friedrich Gärtner: an assemblage containing bastard plants. 7954c225629d4336.title: Geoarchaeological Collection - MUT Tübingen
7954c225629d4336.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. Im Jahr 2006 wurde das Museum der Universität Tübingen MUT als Dachinstitution aller Sammlungen gegründet, zu dessen Aufgaben gehört es, die Betreuung der Bestände in verantwortungsvoller Weise zu organisieren, die Sammlungen für Lehre und Forschung zu öffnen und für nachfolgende Generationen zu bewahren.
7954c225629d4336.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Geoarchaeological Collection The Geoarchaeological Collection is based on a donation from a US-American professor, Paul Goldberg (*1945), who collected sediment samples over decades. The collection contains more than 7000 objects, including thin sections, resin-bound sediment block samples, and sediment casts from archaeological strata and profiles. This makes the collection one of the largest of its kind in the world. The material comes from all inhabited continents and from almost all time periods. Complementing the archaeological objects, the collection contains a large number of recent sedimentary references.Since many of the original sites and profiles no longer exist or can no longer be accessed, the collection represents an invaluable resource for the study of the human past and the evolution of our species. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection). a41cf6e4d20d4a26.title: Geographical Map Collection - MUT Tübingen
a41cf6e4d20d4a26.meta_description: Die mehr als 500 Jahre alte Eberhard Karls Universität Tübingen birgt in ihren 66 Fachsammlungen einen europaweit einzigartigen Bestand. a41cf6e4d20d4a26.text: Search Cart Cart Menu Open/Close Menu Cart UNESCO World heritage [More information] Geographical Map Collection The Geographical Map Collection of the University of Tübingen contains current map sets as well as historical rarities of the nineteenth century. Part of the collection, which focuses on southern Germany, was taken over from the Technical University of Stuttgart. The cultural, industrial, and ecological development of a landscape can be traced on the basis of old map material. Historical maps also document the respective state of the science of land surveying and are therefore important cultural records. Further parts of the Geographical Map Collection are the Wall Map Collection, the Aerial Photograph Collection, the Field Map Collection, the Atlas Collection, and the Atlas Archive. The holdings can be searched via the catalog of the Tübingen University Library. To the collection in the eMuseum (selection).
