b2b2e29dc010301d.text: Athene Grant for female researchers in the postdoctoral phase To promote female scientists in the postdoctoral phase, the University of Tübingen is offering the Athene Grant as part of its university strategy. The Athene Grant is intended to support scientific work, individual career planning, and the acquisition of interdisciplinary skills. Expand all Target group Target group Female scientists (women, trans*, inter*, and non-binary persons) at the University of Tübingen who are in the postdoctoral/habilitation phase or hold a junior professorship. Members of the Faculty of Medicine with a clinical/medical focus (MD) are not eligible to apply. Requirements Requirements Doctorate Scope of funding Scope of funding Total funding: €5,000 per funding year Funding for scientific work: Individual funding for materials, travel, and assistants. Female scientists with family responsibilities have the option of using funding for assistants to compensate for the time spent on family-related tasks. Career planning: This funding priority focuses on individual, professional, and strategic career planning, supplemented by training courses to teach interdisciplinary skills. Workshops/Network: The workshops serve to promote networking and the exchange of experiences among researchers, the acquisition of interdisciplinary skills, and the evaluation and further planning of the Athene Grant program. Participation in the four networking events is mandatory. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle Call for proposals annually, funding period from the beginning of the following year for two years Deadlines Deadlines Application deadline: September 17th, 2025 for funding starting January 1st, 2026

332ceb1b928d7c58.title: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers | University of Tübingen
headings: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers
332ceb1b928d7c58.text: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers The project funding offers postdocs and junior professors at the University of Tübingen start-up financing to establish independent research projects. The project funds are intended to be used for preliminary work on a larger third-party funding application. It is not possible to use the funds to finance one's own position. Expand all Target Group Target Group Postdocs and junior professors at the University of Tübingen Requirements Requirements Eligible applicants are young scientists with a doctorate (including junior professors) at the University of Tübingen who have a position/scholarship at the University of Tübingen until the end of the funding period received their doctorate no more than five years ago do not belong to the Faculty of Medicine Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Applications can be made for Material resources Investment resources Support staff resources NOT: Resources for your own position; basic equipment Application amount: Maximum €35,000 Tender cycle Tender cycle Annually Deadlines Deadlines Application deadline: 15.03.2026

ded1600ec58209c2.title: Tübingen Reloaded | University of Tübingen
ded1600ec58209c2.text: Tübingen Reloaded Tübingen Reloaded is an intramural funding program that enables Tübingen scientists and institutes to invite their international research alumni back to Tübingen for a short stay. The aim of the program is to strengthen ties between hosts and their international alumni, initiate joint activities and collaborations (e.g. research projects and publications) and to establish a global network of research alumni at the University of Tübingen. At the same time, the program aims to increase the international visibility of Tübingen as a research location. Applicants are encouraged to use the alumni invitation as a starting point for an extended stay, which can be financed, for example, by additional third-party funding. Expand all Target groups Target groups Applicants should be senior researchers of the University of Tübingen or affiliated institutes. Invitations can be extended to international alumni/ae (postdocs, professors) who have carried out research at the University of Tübingen for at least 3 months and live and conduct research abroad at the time of application. Requirements Requirements At least three months of research experience at the University of Tübingen The alumnus/alumna must currently be working abroad Independent research profile with at least two years of postdoctoral experience Common research interests between the host and the alumnus/alumna Goal: long-term collaboration and impetus for joint projects with clear prospects Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Successful applicants will get an allowance amounting to the sum that would cover travel and accommodation expenses for up to 7 days. In order to promote long term alumni involvement in current research projects, it is suggested that inviting parties extend the visit to a longer stay with private, departmental or third-party funding. The seed funding can cover travel costs (economy class) on the basis of original travel tickets*. To enable the reasonable completion of official business, rail travel in 1st class is reimbursable, starting at a one-way distance of 100 km. In addition, the cost of the BahnCard can be claimed if amortized. airport transfer by public transport where appropriate: visa costs on the basis of original receipts accommodation in Tübingen (for up to 7 days) with a maximum of 95 Euro (including breakfast) per night.** *For travel connections that deviate from the direct route and are combined with additional private or business appointments not related to the funded project, a comparative offer of the original route must be submitted in order to exclude increased costs due to the alternative routes. Higher costs are to be borne by the customer. **Increased accommodation costs must be justified and documented by alternative offers of further accommodation. Not refundable are upgrades during travel or in accommodation (e.g. seat reservation, excess baggage, use of minibar or upgrade to 2nd person) taxi costs or private transfer, only in those exceptional cases when no public transport alternatives are available travel insurance Ausschreibungsturnus Ausschreibungsturnus Applications can be submitted at any time. Deadline Deadline For a planned research stay, applications should be submitted at least 4 months in advance. Application procedure Application procedure Applications in German or in English should be submitted via an Online-Portal by a current researcher of the University of Tübingen or affiliated institutes and include the following information: application form short description (max. 3 pages): subject, objective(s), long-term perspective and schedule, as well as information about the previous nature, relevance and value of the cooperation timeframe and program activities of the planned visit CV of the inviting researcher and the research alumnus or alumna including proof of previous stay in Tübingen (max. 2 pages each) Furthermore the signed confirmation of conditions and obligations ( download here ) has to be uploaded as a separate PDF-file. Selection procedure Selection procedure Applications should meet all four of the following criteria in order to be assessed on the basis of various further factors: Eligibility: The invited alumnus and alumna (postdocs, professors) has conducted research at the University of Tübingen for at least 3 months, has an independent academic profile and lives and conducts research abroad (research alumni, who completed a doctorate at the University of Tübingen, should have been employed abroad for at least two years). Commitment: The Tübingen host and the research alumnus or alumna have a promising joint research interest and show commitment to a verifiably sustainable cooperation, e.g. new third-party application efforts Quality: The application is clearly structured, comprehensible, and complete. Impact: The funding is used as start-up financing in order to launch new joint activities or intensify existing research cooperation. Reimbursement Reimbursement In order to receive reimbursement for your expenses, please fill out the reimbursement form provided below. Once you have completed the form, please send it along with your receipts to the designated contact person for the scholarship. Reimbursement Form (PDF)

3062ecf363f6af29.title: Research alums | University of Tübingen
headings: Job openings for researchers at the University of Tübingen
In the “Tübingen Reloaded: Insights” series, we interview research alumni who have returned to the University of Tübingen on a Tübingen Reloaded grant.
3062ecf363f6af29.text: Research alumni The University of Tübingen and its institutes host numerous scholars from around the globe – PhD students, visiting lecturers, researchers, professors and postdocs – who engage in various research projects. Following their research stays, TRACe (Tübingen Research Alumni Center) aims to provide research alumni with opportunities to maintain connections at the University of Tübingen and to enhance the alumni network with their diverse expertise and experiences. Dear research alumni, we would like to extend an invitation to join our network and take advantage of our range of opportunities, activities, and events. Please find formats designed specifically for you on this page. Services for research alumni Share your research news The University of Tübingen is proud of its research alumni and the research they conduct. To all research alumni, join the University in celebrating your achievements! We would love to hear more about your latest insights and findings and share them with the UT community and our followers on social media. Send us an email College of Fellows The College of Fellows is dedicated to fostering international and interdisciplinary exchange, both within the University and with the broader public. Learn more about the College of Fellows, the University’s Institute for Advanced Studies for international scholars. Link to the College of Fellows UT Reloaded Scholarship Apply for funding to finance your research stay at the University of Tübingen or to organize alumni meetings abroad. Information on the UT Reloaded Scholarship Fund your research Cutting-edge research requires adequate financial backing. Find out more about funding available to UT research alumni. Details on research funding Job openings for researchers at the University of Tübingen Would you like to continue your career in academia at the University of Tübingen? Current job openings Tübingen Reloaded: Insights In the “Tübingen Reloaded: Insights” series, we interview research alumni who have returned to the University of Tübingen on a Tübingen Reloaded grant. Read further articles Awarded for Research Alumni Strategies The University of Tübingen and TRACe are one of ten winning institutions in the Research Alumni Strategy competition held by the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation. The award which helps us develop and offer new activities is financed with funding provided by the Federal Ministry of Education and Research as part of the " Research in Germany " initiative.

c71b04f97ef642ee.title: International Joint Summer Schools/Workshops | University of Tübingen
c71b04f97ef642ee.text: International Joint Summer School & Workshops The aim of these events is to strengthen the links between the University of Tübingen and its strategically important partners (according to the list of eligible partnerships). The institutional strategy supports Joint Summer Schools and Workshops. The goal is to expand on existing collaborations, to intensify the exchange on joint projects, and to promote the initiation of new projects. List of eligible partnerships > Strategic partnerships > Institutional partnerships Expand all Target Group Target Group Professors and PostDocs of the University of Tübingen who cooperate with academics of strategically important partners of the University of Tübingen. Requirements Requirements Existing cooperation with researchers from strategically important partners of the University of Tübingen The main focus should lie on the exchange on collaborative projects and the plans for long-term consolidation of the cooperation projects Since this is start-up funding that can be awarded once, please describe possible third-party funding formats or other perspectives for sustaining the collaboration beyond the funding period. Selection Criteria Selection Criteria Criterion Weighting 1. Scientific quality of the research project, including ambition, innovative potential, expected results and impact, and prospects for follow up funding (in particular, specifically planned third party funding applications) 30% 2. Clarity of project objectives, project planning, and feasibility of the budget 20% 3. Potential for long term research cooperation, particularly through the inclusion of new departments, disciplines, and research topics within the strategic or institutional partnership 40% 4. Alignment with the strategic research priorities of the University of Tübingen (core research areas and profile areas) 10% Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Funds up to €10.000 are available for the visits of cooperation partners to Tübingen as well as visits of Tübingen academics to the cooperation partners. Invitations/deployments of academics Travel costs (2nd/economy class) Accommodation Catering during the event Student and academic assistants Other necessary material costs for implementation (e.g. printing, marketing, support for online or hybrid formats) For up to 14 days. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle Twice per year Deadlines Deadlines Exception: for the 2026 call, the deadline is 31 May; project activities must be implemented by November 2026. 15 April for activities to be implemented between September and February of the following year 15 October for activities to be implemented between March and August of the following year Application Application Please use the application form to apply and send it via email to: international.research spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de If you have used “artificial intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text and image generation when preparing your application for funding from the Excellence Strategy, please disclose this in your application in a manner appropriate to academic standards and taking into account the specific characteristics of your field. The use of AI in the application will not be viewed as either an advantage or a disadvantage. Applicants remain fully responsible for all AI‑generated content. Further information is available at https://wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Review Process Review Process First, the project proposals are checked for completeness and compliance with the formal criteria. Eligible applications are then reviewed for scientific quality by the dean’s office of the applicant’s faculty. Project and budget planning, the potential for long term cooperation, and alignment with the University of Tübingen’s research priorities are assessed by at least two experts from the Research Division. Evaluation is based on the selection criteria listed above and a grading scale. Based on the consolidated reviews, a funding recommendation is prepared in consultation with the Head of the Research Division. The final funding decision is made by the President’s Office.

2ebd5e4957646e90.title: Innovation Grants (Humanities/Social Sciences) | University of Tübingen
headings: Innovation Grants - Humanities and Social Sciences
2ebd5e4957646e90.text: Innovation Grants - Humanities and Social Sciences Innovation Grants give junior researchers in the Humanities and Social Sciences the opportunity to transfer their research results to practical applications, thus suggesting alternatives to an academic career path within the University. The goal is to apply results attained from doctoral studies to the creation of innovative techniques, services and products through collaboration with extramural partners. This would enable a transfer of theoretical knowledge to the public sphere. Examples of possible projects: Web tools in the context of digital humanities Museum programs, digital exhibitions Pedagogic concepts, e-learning materials, concepts for adult education, schoolbooks, teaching modules for intercultural competence Concepts for refugee aid Concepts for policy and business consultancy (benchmark analysis, case studies, communication plans for specific target groups etc.) Concepts with societal relevance At the end of the funding period a concrete result is expected in the form of a tool, software, an education plan, a consulting or guiding concept etc. which has been developed in collaboration with industrial companies, public institutions or volunteer societies. Expand all Target Group Target Group Doctoral candidates and postdocs at the University of Tübingen Requirements Requirements Applications may be submitted no earlier than six months before or no later than three years after completion of the doctoral degree. The project must be started within six months after approval, otherwise the funding expires. As this is a postdoctoral funding program, funding can only begin after the doctoral procedure has been officially completed with the receipt of the certificate. The application must be supported by a professor in terms of content advice, accommodation and infrastructure. The Innovation Grants are a person-related and not a project-related funding program, i.e. if the funded person leaves the University of Tübingen, the Innovation Grant funding expires. The implementation of the project and the finalization of the transfer must take place at the University of Tübingen and may not be carried out at another institution (with the exception of affiliated institutes). Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Funding is available for junior research positions, student assistants and consumables for 24 months maximum. After this time the project must be finished or have secured external funding. Tender cycle Tender cycle Every six months Deadlines Deadlines The electronic application tool will be activated on February 1st, and August 1st Deadline: February 28th, and August 31st Please respect the deadlines: All required letters and forms with the original signatures should be available by the relevant date and, in addition to uploading in the application database, should be submitted to us in hardcopy form. Submit Your Application to : Applications are to be uploaded into the electronic application tool . In addition, one copy is to be submitted on paper to: Dr. Nora von Ameln, Zentrale Verwaltung, Dez. II, Rümelinstr. 32. wirtschaftskoordination@uni-tuebingen.de 07071 / 29-76812 Any queries about applications can be addressed to the Industry Liaison Office (Wirtschaftskoordination). Application Application Application length max. 6 pages: Cover page with title, name and institute, contact details A detailed description of the project as well as its envisaged practical applications. Please give a precise definition of the project’s goal and emphasize the relevance and the benefits. Please list possible external partners. A timetable for the implementation of the project including milestones A financial plan for the funded period Indication of follow-up funding if applicable (external funding programs…,), further perspectives of the project Attachments CV and publication record Summary of the project, planned procedures, innovative potential of the project; this is for publication - please submit both, German and English Letter of support signed by the supervising professor confirming that he/she will ensure the project has the necessary venue and infrastructure during the funding period. One positive assessment from representatives of your discipline One letter of support from external cooperation partners In case of collaborations with companies, documented serious interest in the project (e.g., Letter of Intent, Letter of Support, ongoing collaboration negotiation, collaboration agreement) Please note that signatures of the Dean and the institute’s director are required for the form “Declaration and fact sheet” (see Downloads ) If a commercial spin-off project is planned, a business plan of about 5 pages is required, which also belongs to the attachment. Notes for the successful development of a business plan can be found here: https://www.existenzgruendungsportal.de/Navigation/EN/Home/home or downloaded from the BIOPRO . Furthermore, the Industry Liaison Office of the University of Tübingen offers consultation previous to applications. It is strongly recommended to make use of the consulting offer in advance. All applications are subject to a formal eligibility check by the Industry Liaison Office upon receipt. If the transfer aspect is not sufficiently considered in the application, the application will not be submitted for review. If you use “Artificial Intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text or image creation in your application for funding from excellence funds, please disclose this in a manner appropriate to academic standards and with consideration for the specific characteristics of your discipline. The use of AI in preparing the application will not be viewed positively or negatively. Applicants bear responsibility for the content generated by AI. More information is available under: https://wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Selection Process Selection Process Applications are reviewed by academic referees and evaluated according to the selection criteria mentioned below. To guarantee that the projects are innovative and feasible the applications will be subjected to a highly competitive selection procedure. The allocation is made by the President´s Office. Selection criteria: Level of innovation Perspectives for the realization and economic exploitability Applicability and sustainability of the envisaged project Scientific quality: Relevance, originality and topicality of the project Currently funded projects Currently funded projects Here you will find a brief introduction to the currently funded projects

62faf45e4d7f4e73.title: Athene Grant | University of Tübingen
headings: Athene Grant for female researchers in the postdoctoral phase
62faf45e4d7f4e73.text: Athene Grant for female researchers in the postdoctoral phase To promote female scientists in the postdoctoral phase, the University of Tübingen is offering the Athene Grant as part of its university strategy. The Athene Grant is intended to support scientific work, individual career planning, and the acquisition of interdisciplinary skills. Expand all Target group Target group Female scientists (women, trans*, inter*, and non-binary persons) at the University of Tübingen who are in the postdoctoral/habilitation phase or hold a junior professorship. Members of the Faculty of Medicine with a clinical/medical focus (MD) are not eligible to apply. Requirements Requirements Doctorate Scope of funding Scope of funding Total funding: €5,000 per funding year Funding for scientific work: Individual funding for materials, travel, and assistants. Female scientists with family responsibilities have the option of using funding for assistants to compensate for the time spent on family-related tasks. Career planning: This funding priority focuses on individual, professional, and strategic career planning, supplemented by training courses to teach interdisciplinary skills. Workshops/Network: The workshops serve to promote networking and the exchange of experiences among researchers, the acquisition of interdisciplinary skills, and the evaluation and further planning of the Athene Grant program. Participation in the four networking events is mandatory. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle Call for proposals annually, funding period from the beginning of the following year for two years Deadlines Deadlines Application deadline: September 17th, 2025 for funding starting January 1st, 2026

2df89a19c06eb417.title: Exchange of Cooperation Partners | University of Tübingen
2df89a19c06eb417.text: Exchange of Cooperation Partners (professorial level) As part of its excellence strategy, the University of Tübingen is specifically strengthening international cooperation with strategically important partner institutions. The aim is to further expand and consolidate existing networks with these partners. In particular, the university promotes mutual exchanges between scientists, who initiate joint (international) third-party funded projects through reciprocal visits, thereby deepening sustainable cooperation between the institutions. List of eligible partnerships > Strategic partnerships > Institutional partnerships Expand all Target Group Target Group Professors of the University of Tübingen who already cooperate with strategically important partners of the University of Tübingen, or would like to initiate new cooperation. Requirements Requirements Cooperation with academics of strategically important partners exist or concrete plans for cooperation are demonstrated During the stay, the focus should be on exchange related to third‑party‑funded research projects Since this is start-up funding that can be awarded once, please describe possible third-party funding formats or other perspectives for sustaining the collaboration beyond the funding period. Selection Criteria Selection Criteria Projects are evaluated based on the following criteria: Criterion Weighting 1. Scientific quality of the research project, including ambition, innovative potential, expected results and impact, and prospects for follow up funding (in particular, specifically planned third party funding applications) 30% 2. Clarity of project objectives, project planning, and feasibility of the budget 20% 3. Potential for long term research cooperation, particularly through the inclusion of new departments, disciplines, and research topics within the strategic or institutional partnership 40% 4. Alignment with the strategic research priorities of the University of Tübingen ( core research areas and profile areas ) 10% Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Funds up to €10.000 are available for the visits of cooperation partners to Tübingen as well as visits of Tübingen academics to the cooperation partners. Invitations/deployments of up to 5 academics Travel costs (2nd/economy class) Accommodation Student and academic assistants Catering for guests in Tübingen in accordance with the guidelines for entertainment costs of the University of Tübingen. Funding for up to 14 days. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle Twice per year Deadlines Deadlines Exception: for the 2026 call, the deadline is 31 May ; project activities must be implemented by November 2026. 15 April for activities to be implemented between September and February of the following year 15 October for activities to be implemented between March and August of the following year Application Application Please use the application form (see download) to apply and send it by email to international.research spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Please attach a brief CV and a list of selected publications from the past five years for the participating researchers to the application form. If you have used “artificial intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text and image generation when preparing your application for funding from the Excellence Strategy, please disclose this in your application in a manner appropriate to academic standards and taking into account the specific characteristics of your field. The use of AI in the application will not be viewed as either an advantage or a disadvantage. Applicants remain fully responsible for all AI generated content. Further information is available at wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Review Process Review Process First, the project proposals are checked for completeness and compliance with the formal criteria. Eligible applications are then reviewed for scientific quality by the dean’s office of the applicant’s faculty. Project and budget planning, the potential for long term cooperation, and alignment with the University of Tübingen’s research priorities are assessed by at least two experts from the Research Division. Evaluation is based on the selection criteria listed above and a grading scale. Based on the consolidated reviews, a funding recommendation is prepared in consultation with the Head of the Research Division. The final funding decision is made by the President’s Office.

044ccd1e51810952.title: Exploration Funds | University of Tübingen
044ccd1e51810952.text: Exploration Funds Exploration Funds are provided for the identification and elaboration of new fields of research and are primarily aimed at disciplines within the Humanities and Social Sciences at the University of Tübingen. The focus should be on research questions that have the potential to significantly shape current or foreseeable social debates. Interdisciplinary collaborations with other disciplines and research areas of the University are expected. The call for proposals for the Exploration Funds 2026 is open to all topics. Exploration Funds 2026 – Call Closed Deadline: March 15 Expand all Target Group Target Group Researchers and interdisciplinary research teams located in the Humanities and Social Sciences Professors and Postdocs (please also see requirements) Requirements Requirements Innovative research project with the potential to be expanded into a third-party funded collaborative project (e.g. CRC/SFB, Research Unit, ERC Synergy Grant) Own previous work on the topic has already been done Interdisciplinary research approach, at least within the Humanities and Social Sciences Proven expertise on the proposed topic must be located in Tübingen An institutional connection is expected The Applicant must have a contract with the university for the duration of the project. At least one person on the main applicant team must be employed on a permanent basis Two letters of interest from internal cooperation partners Scope of funding Scope of funding Start-up funding for a project up to a maximum duration of 3 years, in the form of: Personnel resources (1 postdoc position E13, 100%, teaching obligation 4 weekly hours per semester). An appointment is only possible on a fixed-term basis for qualification purposes (Qualifizierungsbefristung). Consumables (e.g. student assistants, workshops, retreats, seminar series etc.) The actual amount of funding depends on the needs as presented and justified in the proposal Doctoral positions cannot be funded within the framework of the Exploration Funds. Where the state offers start-up funding to support the preparation of applications for DFG formats such as GRK, SFB, TRR, or Research Units, this funding must be used if awarded. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle annually Deadlines Deadlines Application deadline: March 15, 2026 Applications can be submitted via the application database from February 15, 2026. Earliest possible project start: September 2026 Proposal Proposal Requirements and Structure Please use the provided template for the application. The preferred language is English. The application must contain the following information: Cover sheet showing the following information: Details of the applicant, co-applicants, period applied for (earliest start approx. September 2026), total amount applied for, information on whether the application has been submitted earlier in the same or a similar form, and an abstract (max. 100 words) Description of the project (field of research and research questions as well as theoretical and methodological approach), if applicable divided into sub-projects or work packages (max. 8 pages) Bibliography (literature cited in the application) Schematic description of the project plan incl. steps towards submission of the funding proposal Detailed financial plan in tabular form with justification of the requested funds List of associated researchers Short CVs of the (co-)applicants with a list of publications. At least 5 publications relevant to the project should be highlighted. The standard formatting (Arial, 11 pt., line spacing 1.15) must be adhered to, margins must not be changed. The proposal should address, inter alia, the following aspects: Novelty, innovative approach as well as societal relevance and contemporary significance of the proposed project Interdisciplinarity of the project, thematic alignment with existing research areas and structurally embeddedness Internal, national, and international partnerships and networks (existing and planned) for project implementation as well as potential outreach aspects and the integration of non-academic partners Existing preliminary work, the project’s potential for a specific collaborative research framework (e.g. CRC, RTG, ERC Synergy Grant, etc.), and the planned steps towards submitting a corresponding third-party funding proposal Applicant’s qualifications and capacity to successfully implement the project and to develop it into an application for a collaborative third-party funded project (e.g. CRC, RTG, Synergy Grant, etc.) Two letters of interest from internal cooperation partners need to be handed in together with the proposal In addition, please also consider the guidance provided in the template. Application & review process Application & review process Application Applications must be submitted via the University’s Online Application Tool . The application must be submitted in one PDF-Document. The preferred language of applications is English. Incomplete applications cannot be approved for review. Review process First, the project proposals will undergo an administrative eligibility check. All applications that meet the formal requirements will be reviewed by external experts/ peer researchers. Based on the proposals and reviews the Advisory Board for the Humanities will invite the main applicants of selected proposals to present these to the board and afterwards provide a funding recommendation; the final funding decision is made by the President’s Office.

29de4987079bb4c8.title: Faculty Exchange Program with Doshisha University | University of Tübingen
headings: Doshisha EU Campus at the University of Tübingen
29de4987079bb4c8.text: Doshisha EU Campus at the University of Tübingen Research Cooperation with Dōshisha University in Kyoto Faculty Exchange Program The Faculty Exchange Program enables researchers from both universities to undertake short- and long-term research stays at the respective partner institution. The aim is to promote academic exchange and deepen institutional cooperation. Expand all Target Group Target Group Researchers from the University of Tübingen and Dōshisha University Requirements Requirements Teaching obligations in Tübingen must be compensated for during the period of absence. Affiliation with an institute at the host university is planned. Scope of funding Scope of funding The host university covers the accommodation costs for visiting scholars in Tübingen or Kyoto, while the sending university covers the international flight and transfer costs from Tübingen to Kyoto or from Kyoto to Tübingen and back. For short-term stays, a daily allowance will also be paid. Each year, one researcher from each side will be selected for a short-term stay (max. 3 months) and one researcher for a long-term stay (max. 6 months in Tübingen or max. 12 months in Dōshisha). Tender Cycle Tender Cycle At any time Deadlines Deadlines Applications can be submitted at any time. Required information Required information Name of the applicant with affiliation to the institute/faculty of the University of Tübingen CV with list of publications Existing collaboration with Dōshisha University – name of the cooperation partner and institute/center at Dōshisha Planned research: research area, joint research project or project planning (2-3 pages) Description of how teaching obligations in Tübingen will be fulfilled during the period of absence (confirmation by the dean) For postdoctoral researchers, a letter of support from the head of the institute

53103185c95834bf.title: Innovation Grants (Humanities/Social Sciences) | University of Tübingen
headings: Innovation Grants - Humanities and Social Sciences

II, Rümelinstr. 32. wirtschaftskoordination@uni-tuebingen.de 07071 / 29-76812 Any queries about applications can be addressed to the Industry Liaison Office (Wirtschaftskoordination). Application Application Application length max. 6 pages: Cover page with title, name and institute, contact details A detailed description of the project as well as its envisaged practical applications. Please give a precise definition of the project’s goal and emphasize the relevance and the benefits. Please list possible external partners. A timetable for the implementation of the project including milestones A financial plan for the funded period Indication of follow-up funding if applicable (external funding programs…,), further perspectives of the project Attachments CV and publication record Summary of the project, planned procedures, innovative potential of the project; this is for publication - please submit both, German and English Letter of support signed by the supervising professor confirming that he/she will ensure the project has the necessary venue and infrastructure during the funding period. One positive assessment from representatives of your discipline One letter of support from external cooperation partners In case of collaborations with companies, documented serious interest in the project (e.g., Letter of Intent, Letter of Support, ongoing collaboration negotiation, collaboration agreement) Please note that signatures of the Dean and the institute’s director are required for the form “Declaration and fact sheet” (see Downloads ) If a commercial spin-off project is planned, a business plan of about 5 pages is required, which also belongs to the attachment. Notes for the successful development of a business plan can be found here: https://www.existenzgruendungsportal.de/Navigation/EN/Home/home or downloaded from the BIOPRO . Furthermore, the Industry Liaison Office of the University of Tübingen offers consultation previous to applications. It is strongly recommended to make use of the consulting offer in advance. All applications are subject to a formal eligibility check by the Industry Liaison Office upon receipt. If the transfer aspect is not sufficiently considered in the application, the application will not be submitted for review. If you use “Artificial Intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text or image creation in your application for funding from excellence funds, please disclose this in a manner appropriate to academic standards and with consideration for the specific characteristics of your discipline. The use of AI in preparing the application will not be viewed positively or negatively. Applicants bear responsibility for the content generated by AI. More information is available under: https://wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Selection Process Selection Process Applications are reviewed by academic referees and evaluated according to the selection criteria mentioned below. To guarantee that the projects are innovative and feasible the applications will be subjected to a highly competitive selection procedure. The allocation is made by the President´s Office. Selection criteria: Level of innovation Perspectives for the realization and economic exploitability Applicability and sustainability of the envisaged project Scientific quality: Relevance, originality and topicality of the project Currently funded projects Currently funded projects Here you will find a brief introduction to the currently funded projects

18b51c2d274a1b4b.title: Innovation Grants (Sciences/Life Sciences) | University of Tübingen
headings: Innovation Grants in the Sciences / Life Sciences
18b51c2d274a1b4b.text: Innovation Grants in the Sciences / Life Sciences Innovation Grants in the Sciences and Life Sciences make it possible to test the feasibility of translating research findings into applications (proof of concept). Junior researchers (postdocs, straight after completing their PhDs) are to be given the opportunity to translate the knowledge gained in their doctoral thesis into usable processes, services or products. Eligible projects must hold out the prospect that the results will be commercially exploited in the course of the funding. Cooperation with commercial institutions are welcomed as a possible exploitation option, especially since these can open up alternative career paths. However, a serious interest of the company in the proposed project should be documented here (e.g. Letter of Intent - LoI, ongoing cooperation negotiation, cooperation agreement). Projects that aim to conduct a clinical trial cannot be funded within the scope of this program. We explicitly point out that the focus of this funding program is on the economic exploitability and the scientific prospects of success are not primarily in focus. Expand all Target Group Target Group Doctoral students and postdocs who are members or affiliates of the university are eligible to apply, provided that they are also based at the university (see §§7 and 8 of the Grundordnung ) Requirements Requirements Applications may be submitted no earlier than six months before or no later than three years after completion of the doctoral degree. The project must be started within six months after approval, otherwise the funding expires. As this is a postdoctoral funding program, funding can only begin after the doctoral procedure has been officially completed with the receipt of the certificate. The application must be supported by a professor in terms of content advice, accommodation and infrastructure. The implementation of the project and the finalization of the transfer must take place at the University of Tübingen and may not be carried out at another institution (with the exception of affiliated institutes). The Innovation Grants are a person-related and not a project-related funding program, i.e. if the funded person leaves the University of Tübingen, the Innovation Grant funding expires Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Innovation Grants are approved for a maximum of 24 months. After this period, the project must be completed or applied externally. Funding is provided both for the applicant's position and for a staff position or student assistants as well as material and investment funds. Tender cycle Tender cycle Every six months Deadlines Deadlines The electronic database will be activated on February 1st, and August 1st Deadline: February 28th, and August 31st of each year Please respect the deadlines: All required letters and forms with the original signatures should be available by the relevant date and, in addition to uploading in the application database, should be submitted to us in hardcopy form. Contact address for submitting applications: Applications are to be uploaded into the electronic application tool . In addition, one copy is to be submitted on paper to: Dr. Nora von Ameln, Zentrale Verwaltung, Div. II, Rümelinstr. 32 If you have any questions, please contact the Industry Liaison Office: wirtschaftskoordination@uni-tuebingen.de 07071 / 29-76812 Application Application Application length max. 6 pages: Cover page with title, name and institute, contact details Detailed description of the project to turn research results into a commercial exploitation (process, service, prototype, product). Possible external partners must be named, and their expression of interest in the project must be documented (Annex) Objective of funding: long-term cooperation between companies, licensing/patenting, company foundation, etc. Schedule for the implementation of the project, including milestones and financial planning Indication of follow-up financing, if applicable (e.g. external funding programs) Attachments: Abstract: Project summary, action plan, market relevance, the project’s innovation potential (for publication – max. 1 page Arial 11, single-spaced), two versions: German and English Curriculum Vitae and list of publications as well as applicant’s perspective for the future Written confirmation from supervising professor of his/her support and confirmation that he/she will ensure the project has the necessary venue and infrastructure during the funding period Assurance that in case of premature termination of the project, any non-scientific personnel hired will be taken over by the working group for the duration of the contract One positive assessment from representatives of your discipline One letter of support from external cooperation partners - documented serious interest in the project (e.g. Letter of Intent, ongoing collaboration negotiation, collaboration agreement) Form “Declaration and fact sheet” in Downloads filled out and signed by the Dean´s office and the Institute Director If a commercial spin-off project is planned, a business plan of about 5 pages is required, which also belongs to the attachment. Notes for the successful development of a business plan can be found here: https://www.existenzgruendungsportal.de/Navigation/EN/Home/home or downloaded from the BIOPRO . Furthermore, the Industry Liaison Office of the University of Tübingen offers consultation previous to applications. It is strongly recommended to make use of the consulting offer in advance. All applications are subject to a formal eligibility check by the Industry Liaison Office upon receipt. If the transfer aspect is not sufficiently considered in the application, the application will not be submitted for review. If you use “Artificial Intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text or image creation in your application for funding from excellence funds, please disclose this in a manner appropriate to academic standards and with consideration for the specific characteristics of your discipline. The use of AI in preparing the application will not be viewed positively or negatively. Applicants bear responsibility for the content generated by AI. More information is available under: https://wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Selection Process Selection Process Applications will be appraised and approved by academic referees with input from industry consultants. The Grants will be awarded in a competitive process to ensure that the projects have innovative potential and a good chance of success. The award is made by the President´s Office. Please refrain from proposing reviewers for your project. Successful applications will demonstrate: Clear innovative content and economic viability and feasibility Entrepreneurial ability and sustainability Prospects for the realization and sustainability of the project, Academic/scientific quality: relevance, originality and timeliness of the fundamental work Currently funded projects Currently funded projects Here you will find a brief introduction to the currently funded projects.

cdc37755fd4a7ab5.title: International Joint Summer Schools/Workshops | University of Tübingen
cdc37755fd4a7ab5.text: International Joint Summer School & Workshops The aim of these events is to strengthen the links between the University of Tübingen and its strategically important partners (according to the list of eligible partnerships). The institutional strategy supports Joint Summer Schools and Workshops. The goal is to expand on existing collaborations, to intensify the exchange on joint projects, and to promote the initiation of new projects. List of eligible partnerships > Strategic partnerships > Institutional partnerships Expand all Target Group Target Group Professors and PostDocs of the University of Tübingen who cooperate with academics of strategically important partners of the University of Tübingen. Requirements Requirements Existing cooperation with researchers from strategically important partners of the University of Tübingen The main focus should lie on the exchange on collaborative projects and the plans for long-term consolidation of the cooperation projects Since this is start-up funding that can be awarded once, please describe possible third-party funding formats or other perspectives for sustaining the collaboration beyond the funding period. Selection Criteria Selection Criteria Criterion Weighting 1. Scientific quality of the research project, including ambition, innovative potential, expected results and impact, and prospects for follow up funding (in particular, specifically planned third party funding applications) 30% 2. Clarity of project objectives, project planning, and feasibility of the budget 20% 3. Potential for long term research cooperation, particularly through the inclusion of new departments, disciplines, and research topics within the strategic or institutional partnership 40% 4. Alignment with the strategic research priorities of the University of Tübingen (core research areas and profile areas) 10% Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Funds up to €10.000 are available for the visits of cooperation partners to Tübingen as well as visits of Tübingen academics to the cooperation partners. Invitations/deployments of academics Travel costs (2nd/economy class) Accommodation Catering during the event Student and academic assistants Other necessary material costs for implementation (e.g. printing, marketing, support for online or hybrid formats) For up to 14 days. Tender Cycle Tender Cycle Twice per year Deadlines Deadlines Exception: for the 2026 call, the deadline is 31 May; project activities must be implemented by November 2026. 15 April for activities to be implemented between September and February of the following year 15 October for activities to be implemented between March and August of the following year Application Application Please use the application form to apply and send it via email to: international.research spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de If you have used “artificial intelligence” (AI) in the form of generative models for text and image generation when preparing your application for funding from the Excellence Strategy, please disclose this in your application in a manner appropriate to academic standards and taking into account the specific characteristics of your field. The use of AI in the application will not be viewed as either an advantage or a disadvantage. Applicants remain fully responsible for all AI‑generated content. Further information is available at https://wissenschaftliche-integritaet.de/en/use-generative-models/ Review Process Review Process First, the project proposals are checked for completeness and compliance with the formal criteria. Eligible applications are then reviewed for scientific quality by the dean’s office of the applicant’s faculty. Project and budget planning, the potential for long term cooperation, and alignment with the University of Tübingen’s research priorities are assessed by at least two experts from the Research Division. Evaluation is based on the selection criteria listed above and a grading scale. Based on the consolidated reviews, a funding recommendation is prepared in consultation with the Head of the Research Division. The final funding decision is made by the President’s Office.

64b46d0ce669cbba.title: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers | University of Tübingen
headings: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers
64b46d0ce669cbba.text: Program for the Promotion of Junior Researchers The project funding offers postdocs and junior professors at the University of Tübingen start-up financing to establish independent research projects. The project funds are intended to be used for preliminary work on a larger third-party funding application. It is not possible to use the funds to finance one's own position. Expand all Target Group Target Group Postdocs and junior professors at the University of Tübingen Requirements Requirements Eligible applicants are young scientists with a doctorate (including junior professors) at the University of Tübingen who have a position/scholarship at the University of Tübingen until the end of the funding period received their doctorate no more than five years ago do not belong to the Faculty of Medicine Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Applications can be made for Material resources Investment resources Support staff resources NOT: Resources for your own position; basic equipment Application amount: Maximum €35,000 Tender cycle Tender cycle Annually Deadlines Deadlines Application deadline: 15.03.2026

43a77d792938f9d8.title: Summer/Winter Schools | University of Tübingen
headings: Summer & Winter Schools for postdoctoral researchers
43a77d792938f9d8.text: Summer & Winter Schools for postdoctoral researchers In addition to the offers of the Graduate Academy , the funding program primarily serves the qualification of postdocs. The aim of the Summer & Winter Schools is to expand the interdisciplinary and international networking of early career researchers in addition to their scientific and methodological skills. Expand all Target Group Target Group Applications may be submitted by postdocs who are members of the University of Tübingen (see § 7 Grundordnung). Six years after the doctorate (date of the certificate) are counted as the postdoc phase here. Childcare leave is additionally recognized. Requirements Requirements Funding will be provided for 3 to 5 days events addressing scientific topics of interdisciplinary relevance. In view of the international orientation of the event and the desired participation of international guest speakers, it should be conducted in English. The University of Tübingen aims to reduce existing disadvantages for women. For this reason, a balanced gender ratio must be considered when selecting participants. In principle, the planned event should be open to all members of the University of Tübingen. In justified individual cases, the number of participants may be limited. The Summer & Winter Schools may not be applied for to finance existing events or series of events. Scope of Funding Scope of Funding For the implementation of the Summer & Winter School, funding up to a maximum of 12,000 € can be applied as follows: student assistants for the support during the planning, organization and realization of the event (please take employer's contribution into account) travel expenses (flight - economy class, train - 2nd class) and accommodation (max. 95 € per night incl. breakfast) for up to 10 external, actively contributing postdocs Guest speakers Travel expenses (flight - economy class, train - 2nd class) Accommodation (max. 95 € per night incl. breakfast) Catering Coffee breaks Lunch snacks Dinner once per event max. 40,00 € per person only for active contributors named in the program (speakers, moderators, organizers) For online and hybrid events technical equipment and support professional recording, live streaming and archiving moderation The event is to be held within one year of submission of the application. Tender cycle Tender cycle every six months Deadline Deadline 15.03. / 15.09. every year The applications will be evaluated by the University’s Research Commission. Application procedure Application procedure Applications can be submitted via the online application portal Detailed information on how to apply can be found in the internal area (registration with university login). Please submit the form Declaration & Fact Sheet (signed by the head of your institute and your dean) together with the application. You can download the form in the internal area of the Excellence Strategy. Application scope: max. 7 pages

f1c927055950debb.title: Teach@Tübingen Fellowship | University of Tübingen
f1c927055950debb.text: Teach@Tübingen Fellowship The Teach@Tübingen fellowship is a good opportunity for international early career scholars to familiarize themselves with research and teaching at a German university. It aims to expand English-language instruction at the University of Tübingen. For the fellows, the program offers the opportunity to gain teaching experience, to establish international networks, and to broaden their individual research horizons. Expand all Target Group Target Group Postdocs (up to 4 years after completing their doctorate), preferably from strategically important partner universities of the University of Tübingen Focus on researchers whose academic career has mainly taken place outside of Germany Dissertation should not have been completed in Germany Applicants should have worked outside of Germany for the last two years prior to their application Requirements Requirements Affiliation with a host chair Presence in Tübingen during the fellowship Teaching: 2–6 hours per week per semester, in English or a foreign language, must be made public, broad target group, no replacement of basic courses, compulsory attendance Participation in Science Compass, fellow workshop, semester opening of the college of fellows, and certificate ceremony is mandatory Teaching quality is central to the selection process Scope of Funding Scope of Funding 1. Scholarship Postdocs: €1,853/month 2. Teaching assignments 2–6 hours per week per semester Remuneration: €50/hour (for at least 5 students) Payment at the end of the semester 3. Travel expense reimbursement Up to €1,500 for travel to and from Tübingen Reimbursable: economy flight, 2nd class train, public transport No relocation costs or travel expenses for family members 4. Child allowance 1st child: €400/month Each additional child: €100/month Tender cycle Tender cycle Every half year Deadlines Deadlines The next application round is currently being discussed. More Info

72712c446af5d70c.title: Tübingen Reloaded | University of Tübingen
72712c446af5d70c.text: Tübingen Reloaded Tübingen Reloaded is an intramural funding program that enables Tübingen scientists and institutes to invite their international research alumni back to Tübingen for a short stay. The aim of the program is to strengthen ties between hosts and their international alumni, initiate joint activities and collaborations (e.g. research projects and publications) and to establish a global network of research alumni at the University of Tübingen. At the same time, the program aims to increase the international visibility of Tübingen as a research location. Applicants are encouraged to use the alumni invitation as a starting point for an extended stay, which can be financed, for example, by additional third-party funding. Expand all Target groups Target groups Applicants should be senior researchers of the University of Tübingen or affiliated institutes. Invitations can be extended to international alumni/ae (postdocs, professors) who have carried out research at the University of Tübingen for at least 3 months and live and conduct research abroad at the time of application. Requirements Requirements At least three months of research experience at the University of Tübingen The alumnus/alumna must currently be working abroad Independent research profile with at least two years of postdoctoral experience Common research interests between the host and the alumnus/alumna Goal: long-term collaboration and impetus for joint projects with clear prospects Scope of Funding Scope of Funding Successful applicants will get an allowance amounting to the sum that would cover travel and accommodation expenses for up to 7 days. In order to promote long term alumni involvement in current research projects, it is suggested that inviting parties extend the visit to a longer stay with private, departmental or third-party funding. The seed funding can cover travel costs (economy class) on the basis of original travel tickets*. To enable the reasonable completion of official business, rail travel in 1st class is reimbursable, starting at a one-way distance of 100 km. In addition, the cost of the BahnCard can be claimed if amortized. airport transfer by public transport where appropriate: visa costs on the basis of original receipts accommodation in Tübingen (for up to 7 days) with a maximum of 95 Euro (including breakfast) per night.** *For travel connections that deviate from the direct route and are combined with additional private or business appointments not related to the funded project, a comparative offer of the original route must be submitted in order to exclude increased costs due to the alternative routes. Higher costs are to be borne by the customer. **Increased accommodation costs must be justified and documented by alternative offers of further accommodation. Not refundable are upgrades during travel or in accommodation (e.g. seat reservation, excess baggage, use of minibar or upgrade to 2nd person) taxi costs or private transfer, only in those exceptional cases when no public transport alternatives are available travel insurance Ausschreibungsturnus Ausschreibungsturnus Applications can be submitted at any time. Deadline Deadline For a planned research stay, applications should be submitted at least 4 months in advance. Application procedure Application procedure Applications in German or in English should be submitted via an Online-Portal by a current researcher of the University of Tübingen or affiliated institutes and include the following information: application form short description (max. 3 pages): subject, objective(s), long-term perspective and schedule, as well as information about the previous nature, relevance and value of the cooperation timeframe and program activities of the planned visit CV of the inviting researcher and the research alumnus or alumna including proof of previous stay in Tübingen (max. 2 pages each) Furthermore the signed confirmation of conditions and obligations ( download here ) has to be uploaded as a separate PDF-file. Selection procedure Selection procedure Applications should meet all four of the following criteria in order to be assessed on the basis of various further factors: Eligibility: The invited alumnus and alumna (postdocs, professors) has conducted research at the University of Tübingen for at least 3 months, has an independent academic profile and lives and conducts research abroad (research alumni, who completed a doctorate at the University of Tübingen, should have been employed abroad for at least two years). Commitment: The Tübingen host and the research alumnus or alumna have a promising joint research interest and show commitment to a verifiably sustainable cooperation, e.g. new third-party application efforts Quality: The application is clearly structured, comprehensible, and complete. Impact: The funding is used as start-up financing in order to launch new joint activities or intensify existing research cooperation. Reimbursement Reimbursement In order to receive reimbursement for your expenses, please fill out the reimbursement form provided below. Once you have completed the form, please send it along with your receipts to the designated contact person for the scholarship. Reimbursement Form (PDF)

908eb40bb10dfb57.title: Workshops | University of Tübingen
908eb40bb10dfb57.text: Workshops for PhD students In addition to the offers of the Graduate Academy , the funding program primarily serves the qualification of PhD students. The aim of the workshops is to expand the interdisciplinary and international networking of early career academics in addition to their scientific and methodological skills. Expand all Target Group Target Group All PhD students of the University of Tübingen who are members or relatives of the University of Tübingen are eligible to apply (see §§ 7 and 8 Grundordnung). Requirements Requirements Funding is provided for 2 to 3 days events that address scientific topics of interdisciplinary relevance. In view of the international orientation of the event and the desired participation of international guest speakers, it should be conducted in English. The University of Tübingen aims to reduce existing disadvantages for women. For this reason, a balanced gender ratio must be considered when selecting participants. In principle, the planned event should be open to all members of the University of Tübingen. In justified individual cases, the number of participants may be limited. The workshops may not be applied for to finance existing events or series of events. Scope of funding Scope of funding For the implementation of the workshop, funding up to a maximum of 6,500 € can be applied as follows: - student assistants for support during the event days (please take employer's contribution into account) - accommodation (max. 95 € per night incl. breakfast) for up to 10 external, actively contributing PhD students - travel expenses (flight - economy class, train - 2nd class) and accommodation (max. 95 € per night incl. breakfast) for 1 to 2 guest speakers Catering coffee breaks lunch snacks Dinner once per event max. 40,00 € per person only for active contributors named in the program (speakers, moderators, organizers) For online and hybrid events technical equipment and support professional recording, live streaming and archiving Tender cycle Tender cycle Every half year Deadlines Deadlines 15.03. / 15.09. every year The applications will be evaluated by the University’s Research Commission. The event is to be held within one year of submission of the application. Application procedure Application procedure Applications can be submitted via the online application portal Detailed information on how to apply can be found in the internal area (registration with university login). Please submit the form Declaration & Fact Sheet (signed by the head of your institute and your dean) together with the application. You can download the form in the internal area of the Excellence Strategy. Application Scope: max. 5 pages

17adf23268c1fb43.title: Research alums | University of Tübingen
headings: Job openings for researchers at the University of Tübingen
In the “Tübingen Reloaded: Insights” series, we interview research alumni who have returned to the University of Tübingen on a Tübingen Reloaded grant.
17adf23268c1fb43.text: Research alumni The University of Tübingen and its institutes host numerous scholars from around the globe – PhD students, visiting lecturers, researchers, professors and postdocs – who engage in various research projects. Following their research stays, TRACe (Tübingen Research Alumni Center) aims to provide research alumni with opportunities to maintain connections at the University of Tübingen and to enhance the alumni network with their diverse expertise and experiences. Dear research alumni, we would like to extend an invitation to join our network and take advantage of our range of opportunities, activities, and events. Please find formats designed specifically for you on this page. Services for research alumni Share your research news The University of Tübingen is proud of its research alumni and the research they conduct. To all research alumni, join the University in celebrating your achievements! We would love to hear more about your latest insights and findings and share them with the UT community and our followers on social media. Send us an email College of Fellows The College of Fellows is dedicated to fostering international and interdisciplinary exchange, both within the University and with the broader public. Learn more about the College of Fellows, the University’s Institute for Advanced Studies for international scholars. Link to the College of Fellows UT Reloaded Scholarship Apply for funding to finance your research stay at the University of Tübingen or to organize alumni meetings abroad. Information on the UT Reloaded Scholarship Fund your research Cutting-edge research requires adequate financial backing. Find out more about funding available to UT research alumni. Details on research funding Job openings for researchers at the University of Tübingen Would you like to continue your career in academia at the University of Tübingen? Current job openings Tübingen Reloaded: Insights In the “Tübingen Reloaded: Insights” series, we interview research alumni who have returned to the University of Tübingen on a Tübingen Reloaded grant. Read further articles Awarded for Research Alumni Strategies The University of Tübingen and TRACe are one of ten winning institutions in the Research Alumni Strategy competition held by the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation. The award which helps us develop and offer new activities is financed with funding provided by the Federal Ministry of Education and Research as part of the " Research in Germany " initiative.

67e12736d5b5e989.title: Partnerships | University of Tübingen
67e12736d5b5e989.text: International partnerships Transatlantic Scholars Unite on AI Governance Principles The 5th International Interdisciplinary Symposium between Doshisha and Tübingen Held UNC-UT Conference: “Towards a Transatlantic Roadmap for AI Regulation” - Review University of Tübingen and UNC affirm strategic partnership The University of Tübingen strengthens ties with the University of the Witwatersrand UNC-Tübingen partnership wins national IIE Heiskell Award for Strategic Partnerships backwards scroll forward As an internationally oriented research-intensive university, the University of Tübingen engages with its international partner institutions at several levels. Some strategically important collaborations that encompass research cooperation across several faculties and research focus areas are pursued at the institutional level. Partner institutions that cooperate closely with a specific Faculty, form another important pillar of international cooperation. Last but not least, the University also supports its academics in their individual cooperation. The University has traditionally had a wide range of partners in the area of student exchange. Strategic partners Aix Marseille Université Doshisha University Durham University UNC-Chapel Hill University of Nottingham University of the Witwatersrand The University provides intramural funds for research cooperation with its partners of strategic importance. Please find further information at Excellence Strategy – Calls for Proposals . Further important international partners Expand all Institutional Partners Institutional Partners Fudan University Korea University Peking University Universidade de São Paulo The University provides intramural funds for research cooperation with its partners of strategic importance. Please find further information at Excellence Strategy – Calls for Proposals . Core Research Partners Core Research Partners RIKEN University of Tokyo Uppsala University Network Partners Network Partners Matariki Network of Universities ( list of members ) The Guild ( list of members ) CIVIS ( list of members ) Contact International Research Cooperation Rümelinstraße 32 72070 Tübingen international.research spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Website international research cooperation

8eedca56063be258.title: Coaching & Consulting | University of Tübingen
8eedca56063be258.text: Coaching & Consulting Our experienced advisers and coaches are very familiar with the challenges of founders and entrepreneurs and are thus happy to share their knowledge and the wide range of resources of the University of Tübingen with you. We are happy to advise you on the following topics: feedback on and validation of your startup idea search for mentors, coaches or investors advice on funding team building and personality check questions on starting a business and self-employment We will be happy to refer you to suitable contacts in the following matters: advice on starting freelance work or a small business (e.g. for coaching and consulting services, agencies for advertising or software development, etc.) Exemplary advisory process: Duration: approximately one hour Process: you present your idea and receive feedback on the business model, financing options, etc. We discuss next steps Feel free to make an appointment for a free initial consultation! To arrange this, please contact us via e-mail: gruenden spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Any data and information you share with us will be kept strictly confidential. Please send us an outline (approx. one page) with the following information: Do you have a team (names, skills, responsibilities, role in the team aso.)? Which customer problem do you want to solve and which customer demand do you want to address, respectively? What is special about your idea (unique selling point)? Describe your business model briefly This helps us to provide you with optimal advice.

d0d7535a38cbab55.title: Qualification | University of Tübingen
d0d7535a38cbab55.meta_description: With the Doctoral Researcher Development Program (DRDP) for doctoral candidates and the Researcher Development Program (RDP) for post-doctoral researchers, the Graduate Academy offers two qualification programs that are tailored to the needs of researchers in their qualification phases. Our programs address topics such as "Research and Responsibility," "Communication and Management," "Leadership" and "Career Development."
headings: Contact person for doctoral candidates and those interested in pursuing a doctorate
Program Coordinator for Postdocs, Junior Research Group Leaders and Assistant Professors
d0d7535a38cbab55.text: Qualification With the Doctoral Researcher Development Program (DRDP) for doctoral candidates and the Researcher Development Program (RDP) for post-doctoral researchers, the Graduate Academy offers two qualification programs that are tailored to the needs of early- and mid-career researchers (EMCRs). Our programs address topics such as "Research and Responsibility," "Communication and Management, " "Leadership" and "Career Development." Doctoral Researcher Development Program Target group: doctoral candidates and those interested in doing a PhD Research & Responsibility, Communication & Management, Career Development Researcher Development Program Target group: postdocs, assistant professors, junior research group leaders Teaching, Leadership & Management, Career Development Certificate Programs Target groups: from doctoral candidates up to assistant professors Certificate programs on the topics of leadership, science management, science communication and transfer Cooperations Target groups: from doctoral candidates up to assistant professors Qualification Programs and workshops, Consultations and other central facilities at the University of Tübingen Our Qualification Program The registration for our program is on the learning platform ILIAS. You will also find an overview of our current activities and all the information about our program here on our homepage. Link to our Qualification Program Login & registration All information on how to register for our Ilias system can be found here. If you have any further questions on Ilias or the registration for our workshops, please feel free to email us. Registration on Ilias Your Contact Persons Contact person for doctoral candidates and those interested in pursuing a doctorate Contact person for doctoral candidates and those interested in pursuing a doctorate Dr. Martina Bross +49 7071 29-76464 m.bross spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de Program Coordinator for Postdocs, Junior Research Group Leaders and Assistant Professors Program Coordinator for Postdocs, Junior Research Group Leaders and Assistant Professors Dr. Marisa Köllner +49 7071 29-72500 marisa.koellner spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de

a0b14a8de37ef2a7.title: Certificates Programs | University of Tübingen
a0b14a8de37ef2a7.meta_description: Certificates are a sign of additional interdisciplinary qualification. The Graduate Academy currently offers the certificate programs "Leadership in Academia, Industry & Society" and "Science Communication and Media Competence" for graduated researchers.
a0b14a8de37ef2a7.text: Certificate Programs Certificates are a sign of additional interdisciplinary qualification . The Graduate Academy currently offers the certificate programs "Leadership in Academia, Industry & Society" and "Science Communication and Media Competence" in English . Both programs are predominantly targeted at Postdocs, junior professors and junior research group leaders but the programme “Science Communication and Media Competence” also has some availabilities for doctoral candidates. Additionally, we offer two more certificate programs: “Transfer Science and School” and “Science Management”. However, currently, they are only being offered in German. Leadership in Academia, Industry & Society Science Communication and Media Competence Your Contact Person Dr. Marisa Köllner Program Coordinator for Postdocs, Junior Research Group Leaders and Assistant Professors) +49 7071 29-72500 marisa.koellner spam prevention @uni-tuebingen.de

6037ca9cf439efdd.title: Athene Program | University of Tübingen
6037ca9cf439efdd.text: Athene Program The Athene Program at the University of Tübingen aims to support women on their way into academia, starting with their studies and ending with a professorship. In the various program lines Athene Mentoring, Athene Grant and Athene Advanced, offers are made (or still being developed) for different target groups, which are tailored to different career phases (e.g. studies, doctorate, postdoc, junior professorship, NG leadership, etc.) Individual framework conditions and needs (e.g. international women scientists, trans*, inter* and non-binary people, first generation, migration background, care responsibilities ...) study and subject cultures and structures. The program is financed from budget funds and funds from the Excellence Strategy. If you are interested in the Athene Program as a whole or in a specific funding line and would like to be added to the information mailing list, please send a short message via E-mail. Contact and Advice Team Equity - Gender +49 7071 29-74958 +49 7071 29-77766 gender spam prevention @equity.uni-tuebingen.de Responsible Persons Athene Grant Athene Mentoring Athene Advanced
